## 2026-05-31 ŌĆö Wave 6 quick wins shipped: 6.A.1 (transfer fail-loud on drops) + 6.A.9 (DropFk audit trail)

**Resolved (code + L2 climb).** The two immediate Wave-6 confidence-builders are shipped with named acceptance witnesses ŌĆö the first two silent erasures from the five-axis red-team (`AUDIT_2026_05_31_FIVE_AXIS_REDTEAM.md`) are now *closed* (named, not silent), raising the Data and Decision axes off the L1 floor toward L2. The governing discipline held: every erasure became a fail-loud refusal (6.A.1) or a structured diagnostic (6.A.9), never a silent drop.

**6.A.1 ŌĆö `transfer` drops are fail-loud, not exit-0 (Data ŌåÆ L2; red-team Data #1, master-table #1, CRITICAL).** The CLI exited `0` on a successful Execute even when it dropped FK-orphan rows (`SkippedReferences`) or left reconciled-kind sources unmatched (`UnmatchedIdentities`) ŌĆö a refresh script saw "complete" while rows vanished, violating *total decisions, named skips*. The fix is a pure exit-code policy in the Pipeline (`Transfer.exitCodeForReport`, `Transfer.hasDrops`, `Transfer.droppedRowCount`, `Transfer.DroppedReferencesExit = 9`) that the CLI's `runTransfer` consumes, plus a `--allow-drops` override (mirrors `--allow-cdc`) for the operator who has reviewed and declared the loss acceptable. The CLI prints a per-kind drop count to stderr and returns exit `9` (`transfer.droppedReferences`) ŌĆö distinct from connection (6) / reconcile (2) / apparatus (3) so a script can branch on "rows were dropped." Putting the policy in the Pipeline (not the CLI-only `Program.fs`) lets the data canary witness the *same* decision the operator branches on. **Witness:** `` ``data canary: transfer with an unmatched FK exits non-zero (drop is fail-loud, not exit-0)`` `` (Docker canary; reuses the DevŌåÆUAT re-key fixture that drops ghost@x's Order; asserts exit `9` for the drop-set, `0` under `--allow-drops`). Files: `src/Projection.Pipeline/TransferRun.fs`, `src/Projection.Cli/{TransferArgs,Program}.fs`, `tests/Projection.Tests/TransferCanaryTests.fs`.

**6.A.9 ŌĆö `DropFk` decisions surface a Warning (Decision ŌåÆ L2; red-team Decision #2b, master-table #17, MEDIUM).** `foreignKeyStatements` filters out every reference whose key is in `overlay.DropFk` *before* `fkDef` is consulted, so a tightening `Decision` (`DoNotEnforce`) that removed an FK the source enforced never reached `foreignKeyDropDiagnostics` (which only sees *structural* drops ŌĆö unresolved target / target-missing-PK) ŌĆö the decision-driven removal was silent at emission. The fix is a pure sibling of the emitter port, `SsdtDdlEmitter.foreignKeyDecisionDropDiagnostics : DecisionOverlay -> Catalog -> DiagnosticEntry list`, emitting one `Warning` (`decision.fkDropped`, source `emitter:ssdtDdlEmitter`, SsKey = the reference key, structural detail on the typed `Metadata` map) per key in `overlay.DropFk`. Wired into the `runWithConfig` diagnostics aggregation (`Pipeline.fs`) alongside the existing structural witness, so the operator log/manifest names every constraint the engine removed by decision (pairs with `DecisionLogEmitter`). A18 holds ŌĆö `statements`/`emitSlices` stay byte-identical; the audit rides the `Diagnostics` channel, never a `Policy` parameter. **Witness:** `` ``every DropFk decision surfaces a Warning diagnostic`` `` (pure; plus a two-reference "no silent removal" test and an empty-overlay silent-clean test). Files: `src/Projection.Targets.SSDT/SsdtDdlEmitter.fs`, `src/Projection.Pipeline/Pipeline.fs`, `tests/Projection.Tests/DecisionEmissionTests.fs`.

**Verification.** Pure pool green (2513 passed / 208 skipped / 0 failed, ~65s); the 6.A.1 Docker canary passes in isolation (~8s). No regressions. The two known Docker-pool non-regressions (CanaryRoundTripTests A42 2.4 FK-readback container gap ŌĆö 6.A.5's surface; LiveProfilerIntegrationTests B.3.1 resource-pressure flake) are untouched.

**Cross-references:** `AUDIT_2026_05_31_FIVE_AXIS_REDTEAM.md` ┬¦2.2 (Data #1) + ┬¦2.5 (Decision #2b) + ┬¦6 (acceptance catalog) + ┬¦7 (#1, #17); `EXECUTION_PLAN.md` Wave 6 ┬¦6.A.1 / ┬¦6.A.9; NORTH_STAR ┬¦1 (the L1ŌåÆL2 climb). Next on the critical path: **6.A.10 ŌĆö attribute-level `CatalogDiff`** (the structural keystone; nothing in the AŌåÆB story is real without it).

---

## 2026-05-31 ŌĆö Wave 6 keystone shipped: 6.A.10 attribute-level CatalogDiff

**Resolved (code; the structural keystone of the AŌåÆB story).** `CatalogDiff` was **kind-level only** ŌĆö `between` partitioned the `Catalog.allKinds` SsKey set into `Renamed`/`Added`/`Removed`/`Unchanged` but never descended into a kind's attributes. The red-team's single deepest gap (`AUDIT_2026_05_31_FIVE_AXIS_REDTEAM.md` ┬¦1.2, master-table #2, CRITICAL; surfaced independently by Schema, Data-adjacent, and Time): a column change on a name-stable kind (`Email TEXT ŌåÆ NVARCHAR(256)`) produced **no diff signal** ŌåÆ no ALTER ŌåÆ silent full-redeploy with possible type coercion on existing data. "Minimum viable touches" *is* a precise diff; without attribute-level diffing it was structurally impossible. Everything on the AŌåÆB critical path (6.A.11 `applyDiff`, 6.A.12 `diffŌåÆALTER`, 6.A.13 schema CDC-silence, 6.B.2 RefactorLog-aware Transfer, 6.D.1 `migrate`) depends on this.

**The shape (closed-DU expansion; IR-grows-under-evidence).** Three new types in `CatalogDiff.fs`:
- `AttributeFacet` ŌĆö a closed DU naming a single facet of a column's emitted shape: `DataType | Nullability | PrimaryKey | Length | Precision | Scale | Identity | DefaultValue | Computed`. Deliberately mirrors the column-fidelity surface `PhysicalSchema.diff` compares, so the diff and the canary agree on "what counts as a column change." Widen under consumer pressure (ext-props, external storage type).
- `AttributeChange` ŌĆö `{ AttributeKey : SsKey; Facets : Set<AttributeFacet> }`, non-empty by construction (an attribute with no changed facet is `Unchanged`, never `Changed`).
- `AttributeDiff` ŌĆö per kind: `{ Added; Removed : Set<SsKey>; Renamed : Map<SsKey, RenameRecord>; Changed : AttributeChange list }`. `Renamed` carries the edge-case-2 same-SsKey attribute-name change the chapter-3.5 prescope specified; `Changed` is the new facet-level surface. Source-attribute-ordered `Changed` for T1 determinism.

`CatalogDiffData` gains `AttributeDiffs : Map<SsKey, AttributeDiff>` keyed by kind SsKey, **sparse** ŌĆö only kinds present in both catalogs (the kind-level intersection) AND carrying Ōēź1 attribute difference appear. The kind-level partitions are **preserved**: a column change on a name-stable kind keeps the kind in `Unchanged` (the kind-level exhaustiveness contract and every existing consumer ŌĆö `RefactorLogEmitter`, `Lifecycle.evolutionChain`, `CatalogDiffTests` ŌĆö are untouched); the attribute change rides the new map. Accessors `attributeDiffs` / `attributeDiffOf`.

**`isEmpty` strengthened (load-bearing for 6.A.13).** An empty diff now also requires `Map.isEmpty AttributeDiffs`. Before, a kind-name-stable column change reported `isEmpty = true`; now the diff is empty iff the catalogs are structurally identical at both the kind and attribute level ŌĆö the exact predicate schema-level CDC-silence (6.A.13) rests on. Safe for the canary's idempotent-redeploy (same catalog ŌåÆ empty attribute diffs ŌåÆ `isEmpty` stays true; verified ŌĆö `AdjunctionLawTests`/`CatalogDiffTests` green).

**Witness:** `` ``CatalogDiff: a column type change surfaces as an attribute-level Changed entry`` `` (Customer.Name `Text ŌåÆ Integer`; asserts the kind stays in `Unchanged`, the change surfaces as a `Changed` entry naming `DataType`, and `isEmpty` is now honest). Plus: a length-widening test (the `TEXT ŌåÆ NVARCHAR(256)` headline names the `Length` facet), a nullability test, a dropped-attribute test, an identical-catalogs-empty test, and a determinism property. Files: `src/Projection.Core/CatalogDiff.fs`, `tests/Projection.Tests/CatalogDiffTests.fs`. H-043 (`AxiomTests.fs`) updated ŌĆö the attribute-level `Modified` surface it noted as deferred now ships; the remaining gap is the *consuming* delta category (`applyDiff`/`compose`, H-007 / 6.A.11), not the diff surface.

**Verification.** Pure pool green (2519 passed / 208 skipped / 0 failed). No consumer regressions.

**Cross-references:** `AUDIT_2026_05_31_FIVE_AXIS_REDTEAM.md` ┬¦1.2 + ┬¦2.1 (Schema) + ┬¦2.4 (Time) + ┬¦6 (6.A.10) + ┬¦7 (#2); `EXECUTION_PLAN.md` Wave 6 ┬¦6.A.10; `CHAPTER_3_PRESCOPE_REFACTORLOG_AND_CATALOG_DIFF.md` ┬¦2 edge-case-2 (the rename-expansion precedent). Next: **6.A.11** (`applyDiff` + the evolution round-trip law `applyDiff (between A B) A = B`) and **6.A.12** (`diff ŌåÆ ALTER` minimal-touch emitter) both consume this surface.

## 2026-05-31 ŌĆö Wave 6: 6.A.11 applyDiff + the evolution round-trip law (Time ŌåÆ L2; H-007 apply-leg)

**Resolved (code; the Time axis becomes an evolution algebra).** `replayTo` was a snapshot *fetch* (`List.tryFind` by ordinal), not a `fold applyDiff`; `applyDiff` did not exist; `applyDiff (between A B) A = B` was unproven (red-team Time ┬¦2.4, master-table #15). The Time "iso" was store-and-fetch. This slice adds the `between` peer and proves the round-trip law ŌĆö `between` is the *observation* (read a delta), `applyDiff` is the *action* (transform a catalog by a delta); together they make Time an algebra, not a store.

**Faithful, not trivial ŌĆö the no-cheat property.** `CatalogDiff` stores `Source`/`Target` internally, so a na├»ve `applyDiff base d = target d` would satisfy the round-trip law *meaninglessly*. The faithful design: `applyDiff base d` **derives the result keyset from `base ŌŖ¢ delta`** ŌĆö `(keys base \ Removed) Ōł¬ Added` ŌĆö and **transforms `base`'s kinds in place** (kind rename + per-attribute facet patch via `applyFacet`, which moves only the named `AttributeFacet`, so applyDiff's power exactly matches `between`'s detection power). It reads the recorded `target d` ONLY to source genuinely-new content (an `Added` kind's full definition; a `Changed` facet's new value). So the result depends on the passed-in `base`, not just the target. Witnessed by `` ``applyDiff threads the passed-in catalog, not the recorded target (no-cheat)`` `` ŌĆö a diff built from AŌåÆB (Order removed), applied to a *different* base that also carries Country, keeps Country (which the recorded target lacks), proving applyDiff transforms the argument and is not `fun _ d -> target d`.

**The law's modulus (the captured surface).** The diff captures kind presence/name + attribute presence/name + the nine column-shape facets (`AttributeFacet`). It does NOT capture references / indexes / modality / module structure / sequences ŌĆö those ride through from `base` unchanged. The round-trip law is therefore stated order-insensitively via the diff's own equality notion: `` ``Time: applyDiff (between A B) A = B (evolution round-trip law)`` `` asserts `CatalogDiff.isEmpty (between B (applyDiff (between A B) A))` over an AŌåÆB evolving the whole captured surface at once (kind rename + type change + nullability change + dropped attr + added attr). This is more honest than a structural-equality assertion (which would over-constrain on un-captured fields + list ordering); the law's power exactly equals what the diff observes. Plus a direct structural-equality test for the simple in-place facet change (no ordering ambiguity), an identity-diff test (`applyDiff (between A A) A = A`), and a dropped-attribute test.

**The Lifecycle consumer (the algebra made real).** `Lifecycle.reconstructLatest = fold applyDiff genesis (evolutionChain)` ŌĆö replayability as a real *reconstruction*, not a fetch. The exact `replayTo` STAYS (it returns the stored snapshot byte-for-byte, including un-captured facets); `reconstructLatest` is its diff-fold peer and agrees with it modulo the captured surface (`between (latest) reconstructLatest |> isEmpty`, witnessed in `LifecycleTests` ŌĆö and proven non-trivial: the reconstruction is NOT genesis, the rename was applied). Keeping both is the honest move ŌĆö re-basing `replayTo` itself on the lossy-for-un-captured-fields fold would have silently regressed the exact fetch.

**AXIOMS catalog updated.** New citation `` ``6.A.11: applyDiff (between A B) A = B ŌĆö the evolution round-trip law`` `` in `AxiomTests.fs`. Two stale Skips NARROWED, not flipped: **A-Lifecycle-4** (composition associativity) ŌĆö `apply` + diff-replay now land; only the `compose` operator (diffŌłśdiff) remains. **H-007** (SchemaDelta delta-pass category) ŌĆö the `Modified` partition (6.A.10) + `apply` + round-trip law (6.A.11) land; only the delta-pass Kleisli category `SchemaDelta -> Lineage<Diagnostics<SchemaDelta>>` (+ `compose`) remains. Neither flips to Bucket A because each asserts the part not yet built; the trigger narrows and the doc stays honest.

**Scope boundary (deferred, named).** A version-targeted `reconstructTo` (mirror of `replayTo`) wants a "version not found" error in `between`'s `EmitError` algebra, which has no such variant; rather than mix error algebras, only `reconstructLatest` ships. The `compose` operator and the self-contained diff (inline payloads so `Source`/`Target` can be dropped ŌĆö widening the captured surface) defer under IR-grows-under-evidence.

**Verification.** Pure pool green (2527 passed / 208 skipped / 0 failed). No consumer regressions (`replayTo` unchanged; `evolutionChain`, `RefactorLogEmitter`, the canary's `isEmpty` all green).

**Cross-references:** `AUDIT_2026_05_31_FIVE_AXIS_REDTEAM.md` ┬¦2.4 (Time) + ┬¦6 (6.A.11) + ┬¦7 (#15); `EXECUTION_PLAN.md` Wave 6 ┬¦6.A.11; `NORTH_STAR.md` ┬¦1 Time row (L1ŌåÆL2). Depends on 6.A.10 (the attribute-level surface `applyDiff` patches). Next: **6.A.12** (`diff ŌåÆ ALTER` minimal-touch emitter) consumes `AttributeDiffs` to emit `ALTER TABLE ŌĆ” ALTER COLUMN` for `Changed` facets.

## 2026-06-01 ŌĆö Wave 6: 6.A.12 diffŌåÆALTER minimum-viable-touch emitter + column-rename RefactorLog (Time L3-precursor)

**Resolved (code; the engine computes its own minimal delta ŌĆö engine-level, not DacFx-level).** The SSDT emitter emitted **full CREATE TABLE only** (`SsdtDdlEmitter.statements`), so "minimum viable touches" was structurally unsupported ŌĆö the engine redeployed whole tables and leaned on DacFx for idempotence (red-team Time ┬¦2.4, master-table #2). This slice adds the implied **emission differential**: a `CatalogDiff ŌåÆ ALTER` emitter, plus the column-level RefactorLog rename that SSDT requires for data preservation.

**The two SSDT-coupling principles the operator named (held structurally).**
- **RefactorLog is the rename channel.** A column rename emitted as DROP+ADD loses data; SSDT/DacFx needs `sp_rename` via a `SqlSimpleColumn` `.refactorlog` operation (handbook ┬¦"The Silent Catastrophe"). `RefactorLogEmitter` is extended to emit a `SqlSimpleColumn` entry per renamed attribute ŌĆö detected on the **logical `Attribute.Name`** change (`AttributeDiffs[k].Renamed`), mirroring the kind-level logical rename; `NewName` is the new logical name. Per the operator's calls: the refactorlog covers **logicalŌåÆlogical** steady-state renames (the deployed DB already carries logical names ŌĆö V2 emits the logical name as the physical object via `LogicalColumnEmission`), computed over **deployed-state-vs-emitted-state** catalogs (the `migrate` orchestrator feeds read-back-deployed as source, emitted as target; the emitter just projects the diff).
- **Clean-room separation of the two differentials.** `CatalogDiff.between : Catalog ŌåÆ Catalog ŌåÆ CatalogDiff` is the *virtual differential* (a structural comparison of two periodic values); `SchemaMigrationEmitter.emit : CatalogDiff ŌåÆ Diagnostics<Statement list>` and `RefactorLogEmitter.emit : CatalogDiff ŌåÆ ArtifactByKind<RefactorLogEntry list>` are the *implied emission differential*. Distinct functions over distinct types ŌĆö the comparison never knows about emission; the emission never re-derives the comparison. The two emission channels are **disjoint by construction**: a renamed column carries no shape facet (`CatalogDiff` tracks rename and shape on separate axes), so RefactorLog (renames) and SchemaMigration (shape ALTERs) never touch the same attribute. A column both renamed AND reshaped gets a refactorlog rename (oldŌåÆnew) + an ALTER COLUMN on its new name; DacFx applies the rename first.

**`SchemaMigrationEmitter` (the ALTER channel).** New typed `Statement` variants `AlterTableAddColumn` / `AlterTableAlterColumn` + ScriptDom builders (`AlterTableAddTableElementStatement` / `AlterTableAlterColumnStatement`; nullability via the `AlterTableAlterColumnOption.Null | .NotNull` enum ŌĆö **verified empirically against `Sql160ScriptGenerator`** before writing the builder, since guessing the ScriptDom ALTER-COLUMN API risked emitting wrong SQL into the operator's deploy path). The ALTER COLUMN's data-type is built by the *same* `columnDefinition` path as CREATE TABLE (via the new public `SsdtDdlEmitter.columnDefOfAttribute`), so the altered shape is byte-identical to a declared shape ŌĆö or DacFx would re-diff it forever. Wired into `Deploy.executeStream` + `DacpacEmitter.isSchemaStatement` as DDL-class statements. **Safe-additive first slice (T-I faithfulness ŌĆö no silent destructive emission):** emits column ADD + column-SHAPE ALTER (type/length/precision/scale/nullability); narrowing / NULL-tightening is emitted with a `Warning` (`migration.narrowingColumn`, tolerance-gated, surfaced); a dropped column (`migration.destructiveColumnDrop`) and a facet ALTER COLUMN can't express ŌĆö DEFAULT/computed/identity/PK (`migration.unsupportedFacetChange`) ŌĆö are refused fail-loud (`Error` diagnostic, no statement). Added/removed *kinds* are out of scope (a new table is a full CREATE; the `migrate` orchestrator routes those).

**Witnesses.** `` ``migration: a column type change emits an ALTER, not a CREATE`` `` (the headline ŌĆö asserts an `AlterTableAlterColumn`, no `CreateTable`), plus ADD-COLUMN, dropped-column-refused, unsupported-facet-refused, narrowing-Warning, rename-emits-no-ALTER, and a ScriptDom render smoke (`ALTER TABLE ŌĆ” ALTER COLUMN`). RefactorLog: `` ``RefactorLogEmitter: a column rename produces a SqlSimpleColumn entry`` `` + determinism + no-rename-no-entry. **Live canary** (`SchemaMigrationCanaryTests`, Docker): deploys NVARCHAR(50) + a row, emits + executes the widening ALTER against SQL Server, asserts the column is now NVARCHAR(256) AND the row survived ŌĆö the in-place minimal touch preserves data on the operator's real target, not just in the typed model. Two AxiomTests citations added.

**Verification.** Pure pool green (2539 + 9 migration + 3 column-rename = 2551; 0 failed). Live migration canary green (4s). No consumer regressions (the new `Statement` variants lit up exhaustiveness at `DacpacEmitter.isSchemaStatement` + `Deploy.executeStream` ŌĆö both covered as DDL arms).

**Deferred (named).** DEFAULT-constraint ALTER DDL (`ALTER TABLE ŌĆ” ADD/DROP CONSTRAINT ŌĆ” DEFAULT`), computed-column and identity changes (need DROP+ADD or are non-alterable), and `DROP COLUMN` / `DROP TABLE` (destructive) are refused fail-loud now; each lands when the `migrate` orchestrator (6.D.1) needs it under an explicit operator-declared-destructive gate. The kind-level RefactorLog still keys on logical `Kind.Name` (existing, untouched); the column-level matches it (logical) ŌĆö consistent.

**Cross-references:** `AUDIT_2026_05_31_FIVE_AXIS_REDTEAM.md` ┬¦2.4 (Time) + ┬¦6 (6.A.12) + ┬¦7 (#2); `EXECUTION_PLAN.md` Wave 6 ┬¦6.A.12; `handbook/09-The-Refactorlog-and-Rename-Discipline.md` (the SSDT rename semantics); `CHAPTER_3_PRESCOPE_REFACTORLOG_AND_CATALOG_DIFF.md` (the RefactorLog format). Depends on 6.A.10 (the attribute-level diff). Next: **6.D.1** (`migrate A B`) composes `CatalogDiff.between` (deployed-state vs emitted-state) ŌåÆ RefactorLog renames + SchemaMigration ALTERs + transactional transfer + verify.

## 2026-06-01 ŌĆö Wave 6 ontology + premise re-prioritization (the shape of change over time)

**Resolved (vision/ontology-level; the masterwork that grounds Wave 6 in the operator's physical premise).** A multi-turn reasoning session traversed the microscope from the categorical frame down to the physical floor (pages/locks/CDC/identity) and back up through the operator's concrete operational reality. The deliverable is `WAVE_6_ONTOLOGY.md` ŌĆö the full-fidelity source for the premise and the change-ontology; this entry + the `EXECUTION_PLAN.md` Wave 6.F sub-wave are its projections (mirrors how `AUDIT_2026_05_31` projects into NORTH_STAR/EXECUTION_PLAN).

**The organizing thesis.** The engine is *right-by-function* exactly when its structure is **isomorphic to the core moves of change over time** (Add / Remove / Rename / Reshape / Reidentify / Move / Accumulate ŌĆö `WAVE_6_ONTOLOGY.md` ┬¦5). A name is a promise about a function; a test that exercises only the name's *expected* behavior confirms the promise's surface, not its substance ŌĆö so a wrong implementation that shares the name's surface passes. Two live near-misses from this very wave are the evidence: `applyDiff` returning the stored target (right by name, trivially satisfies the round-trip law, ignores its argument) and "physical column rename" detecting `Column.ColumnName` (right by name, wrong name-space). The structural remedy is the isomorphism + a **discriminating predicate** per entity (the input on which a plausibly-named wrong version diverges ŌĆö e.g. `applyDiff`'s no-cheat `Ōłā base'ŌēĀsource. apply(d,base')ŌēĀtarget(d)`). This recurses the five-axis red-team's method down to each abstraction.

**The premise, corrected (the L1 operational layer ŌĆö `WAVE_6_ONTOLOGY.md` ┬¦2).** This is a **publication-and-provenance engine for an evolving relational model**, not a deployment engine for a live regulated PROD:
- **Consumers:** an external team's SSIS jobs mapping a *legacy* database into *this* schema's shape (they track the model's evolution over time), the own Dev/QA on-prem databases, and the terminal **eject** (schema freeze ŌåÆ the model stops being OutSystems-derived).
- **Flows:** forward-only ŌĆö cloudŌåÆon-prem (Dev, QA), and DevŌåÆUAT with **user re-key** (the `UAT-users` command). **PROD has no application/data yet.** No masking (Dev/QA data).
- **Cadence:** once a sprint, **demand-driven by the consuming team** ŌĆö lumpy, not high-frequency.
- **The why:** OutSystems' own deploy is opaque (*trust me*); this is a parallel SQL-native, repo-tracked, PR-reviewed, *provable* and *ejectable* control plane.

**The load-bearing distinctions the ontology pins (each tagged law/policy/target, anchored to a witness or trigger):**
- **Two differentials, never conflated:** the *comparison* (`CatalogDiff.between`, observational) vs the *implied emission* (the plan, operational). Plus the **intent filter** the operational zoom reveals ŌĆö a *raw observed difference is not a true delta* (DacFx normalization, auto-named constraints, read-back gaps inject noise); the pipeline is `observe ŌåÆ raw difference ŌåÆ filter(tolerance ŌŖĢ operator-intent) ŌåÆ true delta ŌåÆ plan`. This is pillar 9 in operational form: every observed difference is operator-intended (emit) or substrate-noise (tolerate); silence on neither.
- **Three name-spaces:** Identity (`SsKey`, invariant) / Designation (`Name`, renames act here) / Realization (`Column.ColumnName`/`Physical.Table`). **[policy] `Realization := Designation`** (V2 emits the logical name AS the physical object, default-on). The rename predicate falls out: a rename is needed iff *deployed Realization ŌēĀ emitted Realization* for the same Identity ŌĆö which, under the policy, is logicalŌåÆlogical, computed over deployed-state-vs-emitted-state. (This is why the column-rename detection is on logical `Attribute.Name`, not physical `ColumnName`.)
- **The DacFx-delegation seam [policy]:** schema plan is *delegated* (engine emits the declarative CREATE + refactorlog; DacFx computes the ALTER at publish) ŌĆö "prefer not to emit ALTER statements"; data plan is *engine-owned* (pre/post-deployment scripts). So `SchemaMigrationEmitter`'s explicit ALTER (6.A.12) is **repositioned**: a preview/verify/measure *lens*, not the deploy artifact.
- **Two emission modes [policy], both first-class:** fresh-replacement (drop+load; always correct, never minimal ŌĆö safety baseline) and incremental (CREATE + refactorlog-against-prior + data scripts; minimal, CDC-measured).
- **CDC is the ruler [instrument], not a constraint:** the `cdc.<table>_CT` capture-row count *is* the data-movement count; the operator uses it to track/enforce the minimum viable data movements to arrive at current-A from an earlier-A's provenance. P-DM: `capture count = |true data delta|`; `CdcSilenceTests` is the `|delta|=0` floor.

**The premise re-prioritization (reorders the audit's generic critical path).** **Deferred-with-trigger** (PROD empty): 6.C.2 (transactional/resumable) + 6.C.1 (permission/connection gates) ŌĆö *trigger: PROD gains data or a write-denied environment enters a real flow.* **Repositioned:** 6.A.12 explicit ALTER ŌåÆ lens. **Up (new ŌĆö `EXECUTION_PLAN.md` Wave 6.F):** 6.F.1 refactorlog-against-prior (Accumulate/provenance), 6.F.2 two-mode emission, 6.F.3 data-movement post-deploy script + CDC-count enforcement (P-DM general case), 6.F.4 published-model + provenance surface for the SSIS consumer/eject. **Held:** 6.B.1's live form is the DevŌåÆUAT rekey compatibility; 6.D.1 `migrate` remains the composition under the ordering/partition laws.

**Defused (per the premise):** physical names are not reused **and** provenance compares engine-emitted snapshots (true `SsKey`s, not synthesized-from-name) ŌĆö so endpoint-diff is sufficient; name-reuse ambiguity cannot bite; the synthesized-key bound (`AUDIT` ┬¦2.3 / 6.A.7) holds in this estate precisely because names are stable+unique. Path-history matters for *provenance/replay*, not *disambiguation*.

**Decisions owed (named-with-trigger; `WAVE_6_ONTOLOGY.md` ┬¦10):** (1) is the prior `.refactorlog` an engine input at emit time or a repo-merge concern (gates 6.F.1); (2) what does the SSIS team consume ŌĆö dacpac / per-sprint changelog / machine-readable diff (gates 6.F.4); (3) at eject, frozen state + full refactorlog history or state alone (decides P-PROV append-forever vs collapsible).

**Grounding the totalities (`WAVE_6_ONTOLOGY.md` ┬¦9).** T-I quantifies over the comparison (P-CMP) + the emission functor's partiality (faithful/lossy-warn/refuse); T-V over the channels (P-CH partition + P-ORD ordering); T-VI over the gates (P-GATE completeness = "spanning"). The ontology supplies the entities the totalities range over.

**Cross-references:** `WAVE_6_ONTOLOGY.md` (the masterwork; the deep map); `EXECUTION_PLAN.md` Wave 6 + 6.F (the route); `AUDIT_2026_05_31_FIVE_AXIS_REDTEAM.md` (why the climb exists); `NORTH_STAR.md` ┬¦3 (the totalities this grounds). Reading-order amended (CLAUDE.md) to place `WAVE_6_ONTOLOGY.md` alongside the audit as a Wave-6 first-read.

## 2026-06-01 ŌĆö Wave 6 ontology: the data-leg addendum (CDC-aware minimum data diff)

**Resolved (ontology addendum; `WAVE_6_ONTOLOGY.md` ┬¦12).** Extends the change-ontology from schema to **data** ŌĆö *how data is coerced to change over time*. The data leg is the **same shape of change one plane down**: the data moves are the row-level analog of the schema moves (┬¦5 Ōłź ┬¦12.3) ŌĆö **Insert / Update / Unchanged(=silence) / Delete / Reidentify**. The operator's stated preference (CDC-aware minimum data diff over a complete replace) is the data-plane reading of "minimum viable touches."

**Load-bearing parallels pinned (the ┬¦12.0 correspondence table):**
- **Comparison:** `CatalogDiff.between` (schema) Ōłź the row diff (data). The data comparison has two regimes ŌĆö engine-computed at-source vs **MERGE change-detection at-target** ŌĆö and **[policy] the at-target MERGE is the CDC-aware-minimum default** (the engine emits *the statement that is the diff*; SQL Server computes the delta at apply; CDC observes exactly the writes; no read of the live target needed).
- **Delegation:** schema ALTER is *delegated to DacFx*; **data is 100% engine-owned** (DacFx does not move data) ŌåÆ the data diff is the engine's hardest-owned correctness, with CDC as its only external ruler.
- **Provenance/ruler:** append-only refactorlog (schema) Ōłź **the append-only CDC capture log** (data) ŌĆö simultaneously the provenance (replays to reconstruct the data evolution from an earlier-A) and the ruler (the operator reads it to verify the deploy moved exactly the minimum). P-DM: `capture count = |true row delta|; idempotent ŌćÆ 0`.
- **Emission modes:** fresh-replace (TRUNCATE+bulk reload; `2├Ś|table|` captures; safety baseline) ŌŖĢ CDC-aware minimum diff (the change-detecting MERGE; preferred), selected by the same `EmissionMode` as schema.

**Right-by-function anchor.** The CDC-aware-minimum MERGE's correctness is *not* "it's a MERGE." The heart is the **change-detection predicate** ŌĆö the null-safe distinctness `(Target.c <> Source.c) OR (Target.c IS NULL AND Source.c IS NOT NULL) OR (Target.c IS NOT NULL AND Source.c IS NULL)` over the comparable columns (`ScriptDomBuild.perColumnChangeDetection`), NOT a naive `<>` (which is `UNKNOWN` on NULL ŌåÆ misses NULLŌåövalue transitions) and NOT an unconditional `WHEN MATCHED` (captures every matched row ŌåÆ `|matched|` not `|changed|`). Plus: the `ON` keys on **reconciled identity** not raw surrogate (else a re-keyed row splits into Delete+Insert); the comparable-column-set **excludes** computed + reconciled-surrogate + tolerated columns; the `DELETE` clause is **scope-gated**. Each is a discriminating predicate with a wrong-by-name failure (`WAVE_6_ONTOLOGY.md` ┬¦12.4, ┬¦12.7).

**Built + witnessed:** `changeDetectionPredicate` (null-safe); CDC-silence floor (`CdcSilenceTests` Slice ╬│ + sensitivity; `CdcSilenceCrossEmitterTests` C0ŌĆōC4); reconciled re-key + two-phase FK + `DataLoadPlan` ordering; transfer CDC pre-flight; drop fail-loud (6.A.1). **Ō¼Ü (route in `EXECUTION_PLAN.md` 6.F.3-data):** P-DM general case (`capture=k`); the empty/NULL + padding/scale tolerance (6.A.4 + P-DIFF hardening); the DELETE-scope gate (P-DEL-SCOPE); the data-provenance surface; P-ATOMIC-DATA (deferred, PROD empty).

**Decision owed (data leg):** is the incremental data plan a *post-deployment script* (SSIS-consumer/eject publication flow) or a *transfer-verb execution* (DevŌåÆUAT rekey)? They share the MERGE algebra but differ in artifact + ordering. Likely both; name the seam before 6.F.3-data(a) emits.

**Cross-references:** `WAVE_6_ONTOLOGY.md` ┬¦12 (the addendum); `EXECUTION_PLAN.md` Wave 6.F.3-data; `AUDIT_2026_05_31` ┬¦1.3 (CDC-silence is the one genuinely-solid result) + Data #4 (empty/NULL); `DECISIONS 2026-06-01 ŌĆö Wave 6 ontology + premise re-prioritization` (the schema-leg parent entry this extends).

## 2026-06-01 ŌĆö Wave 6 reified into the change algebra (T12ŌĆōT16 + A43; State is a torsor over Delta)

**Resolved (formal-system level; the masterwork reification).** The change-ontology (`WAVE_6_ONTOLOGY.md`, both legs) is reified into the **domain's algebra** ŌĆö `WAVE_6_ALGEBRA.md` (the calculus) + the numbered theorems **T12ŌĆōT16 + A43** in `AXIOMS.md`, each an executable entry in `AxiomTests.fs`. The brief was to express the laws as *balanced equations with variables in their most revealing native form*; the native form is an **affine space (torsor)**.

**The revealing move ŌĆö State is a torsor over Delta.** A `Delta` is not a "diff record" ŌĆö it is the *difference of two States*. Postulate `ŌŖ¢` (= `CatalogDiff.between`, subtraction: `╬┤ = B ŌŖ¢ A`) and `ŌŖĢ` (= `CatalogDiff.applyDiff`, the affine action: `B = A ŌŖĢ ╬┤`). Then **the round-trip, identity, and composition laws are not three facts ŌĆö they are the three Weyl axioms of an affine space**, so they balance *by construction*, not by assertion. This is why the equation balances and why the variables are in native form: change is *displacement*, a database state is a *point*, the move-alphabet generates the displacement group, the change-measure is the norm, and emission is a norm-preserving functor realizing the action on the substrate.

**The theorems (each a balanced equation; witness or trigger):**
- **T12 ŌĆö torsor axioms.** `A ŌŖĢ (B ŌŖ¢ A) = B`; `A ŌŖĢ 0 = A`; `(AŌŖĢ╬┤Ōéü)ŌŖĢ╬┤Ōéé = AŌŖĢ(╬┤Ōéü+╬┤Ōéé)`. State-dependence is *forced* by W3 uniqueness ŌåÆ the no-cheat law (`applyDiff base d = target d` violates it). Ō£ģ witnessed (round-trip + no-cheat + identity-diff).
- **T13 ŌĆö evolution over time.** `replay(t) = genesis ŌŖĢ ╬Ż╬┤` = `fold ŌŖĢ` (Chasles along the timeline). Ō£ģ `reconstructLatest`; Ō¼Ü the `compose` operator (A-Lifecycle-4 / H-007).
- **T14 ŌĆö orthogonality as a direct sum.** `╬┤ = ŌŖĢ_c ŽĆ_c(╬┤)`, disjoint+covering, `ŌĆ¢╬┤ŌĆ¢ = ╬ŻŌĆ¢ŽĆ_c ╬┤ŌĆ¢`. **Subsumes A38** (kind-level partition); generalizes to attribute + data planes. T-V *is* this decomposition. Ō£ģ A38 + RenameŌŖźReshape.
- **T15 ŌĆö CDC is the norm; emit is an isometry.** `ŌĆ¢╬┤ŌĆ¢_data = |capture|`; `ŌĆ¢emit(╬┤)ŌĆ¢ = ŌĆ¢╬┤ŌĆ¢`. CDC-silence = the `ŌĆ¢╬┤ŌĆ¢=0` instance; minimum data diff = isometric emission; complete-replace = non-isometric (`2┬Ę|table|`) ŌåÆ the fallback. Ō£ģ CDC-silence floor + sensitivity; Ō¼Ü general `ŌĆ¢╬┤ŌĆ¢=k` (6.F.3-data).
- **T16 ŌĆö the Project square (master equation).** `run(emit(BŌŖ¢A), realize(A)) = realize(B)` modulo (erasure ŌŖÄ tolerance) ŌĆö H-050's adjunction lifted from points to displacements; schema + data legs are its two projections; the iso-ladder L1/L2/L3 is the totality/faithfulness of `emit`. ŌŚæ sub-squares witnessed; Ō¼Ü the full square = the `migrate A B` canary (6.D.1). Bucket C until then.
- **A43 ŌĆö Identity is the conserved charge.** `SsKey` conserved under every move (Add creates, Remove annihilates); Rename perturbs Designation conserving Identity; `Realization := Designation` (policy). **Cross-plane corollary ŌĆö the algebraic *why* of the refactorlog:** a faithful schema Rename induces `ŌĆ¢┬ĘŌĆ¢_data = 0` (sp_rename conserves rows), DROP+ADD induces `2┬Ę|table|` ŌĆö so the refactorlog is *forced* by Identity-conservation across the schemaŌåÆdata coupling, not adopted as convention. Ō£ģ column-rename + re-key; Ō¼Ü `ŌĆ¢renameŌĆ¢_data=0` canary.

**Relationship to the prior catalog (deepen + unify; none superseded):** T16 = H-050 lifted to arrows; T14 subsumes A38; T15 generalizes the chapter-4.1.B CDC-silence property to a norm-conservation law; A43 generalizes A1 (identity-survives-rename) to all moves and *derives* the SSDT refactorlog requirement; A35/A36 describe `emit`'s codomain and A36's bulk-vs-incremental is T15's isometric-vs-non-isometric reading.

**Why this is the right reification (right by function, not by name):** every law is an equation `LHS = RHS` in the carriers' native operations (`ŌŖĢ ŌŖ¢ + ŌĆ¢┬ĘŌĆ¢ ŽĆ emit realize`) ŌĆö no one-sided assertion. The torsor framing fuses round-trip/identity/composition into one structure (you cannot state one without the others), which is the "most revealing native expression." CDC = the norm makes "minimum viable touches" a measured equality. T16 is the single master equation; the others are its facets; the schema and data legs are its projections; the iso-ladder is its faithfulness gradient ŌĆö the engine is right-by-function exactly when these balance with the smallest residual.

**Verification.** AxiomTests green (79 live / 8 C / 1 D; the 5 new Fact theorems + T16 Skip-with-trigger); matrix gate PASS, round-trip 5/5; all 9 cited witnesses verified present.

Extends `DECISIONS 2026-06-01 ŌĆö Wave 6 ontology + premise re-prioritization` and `ŌĆ” the data-leg addendum`.

## 2026-06-01 ŌĆö Activating the calculus into the codebase (EXECUTION_PLAN 6.G), holding the spine

**Resolved (route-level; the weave back into the buildable plan).** With the change algebra reified (`WAVE_6_ALGEBRA.md`; `AXIOMS.md` T12ŌĆōT16 + A43), the question "what must be done to activate the calculus in code while holding the spine" is answered as `EXECUTION_PLAN.md` **┬¦6.G** ŌĆö which does **not** add a parallel plan but **reframes the remaining Wave 6 slices as the activation of named theorem-residuals.** A theorem is *activated* when its operations are first-class **and** its residual (the Ō¼Ü) is closed by its **discriminating witness**, flipping its `AxiomTests.fs` entry.

**The spine-holding discipline (the load-bearing decision).** **Activation is behavioral, not structural ŌĆö until the second consumer.** Most activation is closing residuals with witnesses, not restructuring code. The **type-level torsor surface** (a shared `Delta` / `ŌŖĢ` / `ŌŖ¢` / `ŌĆ¢┬ĘŌĆ¢` / `ŽĆ` / `emit`) is extracted **only when the data leg becomes the second consumer** of the comparisonŌåÆapplyŌåÆemit pattern (slice 6.F.3-data / 6.G.2) ŌĆö per the two-consumer threshold + anticipation-vs-speculation **Position B**, not before. **The spine-breaker to refuse: the speculative torsor refactor** ŌĆö renaming `between`ŌåÆ`ŌŖ¢`, introducing a `Delta` supertype, or instantiating a `Torsor`/`AffineSpace` abstraction ahead of the second consumer. The algebra is the **spec the witnesses check**, not a shape to force the code into; right-by-function means the code *behaves* like the torsor (proven by the discriminating witness), never *named* like it on speculation. This is the one place the calculus could seduce a scope-widening, and the discipline forecloses it ŌĆö consistent with "IR grows under evidence" and the two-consumer threshold.

**The activation critical path (premise-respecting):** 6.F.1 (refactorlog-against-prior ŌåÆ A43/T13 provenance) ŌåÆ 6.F.3-data (CDC-aware MERGE over arbitrary deltas + the `ŌĆ¢╬┤ŌĆ¢=k` canary ŌåÆ T15 general; **the second consumer** where the shared `Delta` surface earns structural extraction) ŌåÆ 6.A.4 + data P-DIFF (intent-filter ŌåÆ T16 residual) ŌåÆ 6.G.3 (the `ŌĆ¢renameŌĆ¢_data=0` cross-plane canary ŌåÆ A43 corollary made live) ŌåÆ 6.D.1 (`migrate A B` ŌåÆ **T16**, the master equation, under T14 partition + T13 ordering, gated by the live DevŌåÆUAT data-compat, not PROD). Per-slice rent unchanged: the witness named to its matrix substring, the AxiomTests entry flipped, matrix regenerated, a DECISIONS cash-out, the load-bearing commitments (A18 / pure Core / writer-fidelity / pillar 9) held, the premise re-prioritization (PROD-gates deferred) held.

**End-state:** when every residual is zero, T16 is green (the migrate canary), its AxiomTests entry flips SkipŌåÆFact, the matrix reports L3, and the calculus is *enforced* ŌĆö a regression that breaks any equation drops its AxiomTests entry and a matrix cell, loudly. That is `NORTH_STAR` Promise 8, stated as "every equation balances."

## 2026-06-01 ŌĆö Wave 6 morphology: the calculus is latent; the concern-movement field; multi-episodic substrate

**Resolved (concrete-grounding level; the research-and-unify undertaking).** Four dedicated read-only research agents mapped the codebase *as it is* against the ontology/algebra (which had been written *over* the code, not *from* it). The unification is `WAVE_6_MORPHOLOGY.md` (the territory the map was drawn over); the novel inferences are folded into `WAVE_6_ALGEBRA.md` ┬¦12 and the `EXECUTION_PLAN.md` 6.G/6.H route. Agents: (1) amino acids ŌĆö the structural primitives; (2) proteins ŌĆö the concrete use cases; (3) concern-movement through emission space; (4) multi-episodic recombination over time.

**The central finding ŌĆö the calculus is *latent*, not *activated*.** Four convergent projections of one truth:
- **Carriers reified; verbs not.** 7 of 8 amino-acid families are production-grade (`Catalog`/`PhysicalSchema`, `CatalogDiff`, `Lifecycle`, `DecisionOverlay`, the writer trinity, the realization stack). **But the operators-as-types have no code home:** no `Move`, no abstract `Delta`, no norm `ŌĆ¢┬ĘŌĆ¢`, no channel `ŽĆ`, no `Torsor`. The codebase types the *nouns* of change; the *verbs* are functions (`between`/`applyDiff`) + test-witnessed laws.
- **The diff-machinery is orphaned.** `between`/`applyDiff`/`SchemaMigrationEmitter`/`RefactorLogEmitter`/the whole of `Lifecycle` have **zero production callers** outside provenance-replay in tests ŌĆö "amino acids with no protein." Every shipping use case is single-leg; no orchestrator; renames flow into emit but never into transfer.
- **The engine ships `realize(B)`, not `emit(B ŌŖ¢ A)`.** The integrated path has no `CatalogDiff` input; the displacement ╬┤ is computed by no wired path; the differential leg of T16 is dark. The manifest is an integral over *state*, not displacement or time.
- **No durable episode to integrate over.** `reconstructLatest` (the FTC `genesis ŌŖĢ ╬Ż╬┤`) runs only over in-memory test values; `CatalogSnapshot` is schema-only, single-plane, time-erased, ephemeral; only `V2.SsKey` survives an episode boundary (as a current value, not a chain).

**Novel inferences folded into the calculus (`WAVE_6_ALGEBRA.md` ┬¦12):**
1. **The concern-movement field** ŌĆö a concern ╬║ moves in a 2-D field: `Ōłé╬║/Ōłé(emission)` [which artifact; integrated by the manifest] and `Ōłé╬║/Ōłé(episode)` [which time-step; integrated by the provenance/LifecycleStore; the FTC T13 is this integral recovering state]; the mixed partial `Ōłé┬▓╬║/Ōłé(emission)Ōłé(episode)` is the change-manifest series. "Observe concern movement during multi-episodic recombination" = observe `Ōłé╬║/Ōłé(episode)` + the cross-concern join over a multi-plane episode store. Today only the `Ōłé╬║/Ōłé(emission)`-of-state slice is lit; Time is wholly dark.
2. **Latent vs activated** ŌĆö a law is *latent* when proven in isolation but unwired/un-persisted; *activated* when wired + durable. T13 (no durable episode) and T14/T15 (no `ŽĆ`/`ŌĆ¢┬ĘŌĆ¢` carrier) are latent, not merely "Ō¼Ü trigger." AXIOMS T13/T15 status sharpened to say so + name the activation triggers.
3. **The noun/verb reification principle** ŌĆö carriers reify eagerly; operator-verbs (`Move`/`Delta`/`ŌĆ¢┬ĘŌĆ¢`/`ŽĆ`/`Torsor`) reify at the **second consumer** (the data `RowDiff` + `migrate`), never on speculation. **The spine-breaker to refuse: the speculative torsor refactor** (renaming `between`ŌåÆ`ŌŖ¢`, a `Torsor` typeclass) ahead of the second consumer. The verbs are absent because the second consumer hasn't been built, not by oversight ŌĆö so their absence is *correct discipline*, and `EXECUTION_PLAN.md` ┬¦6.G's "structural activation at the second consumer" is now grounded in *why*.

**The future state (multi-episodic observability; `EXECUTION_PLAN.md` 6.H, grounded in proven amino acids):** the multi-plane `Episode` (co-records schema+profile+refactorlog+CDC at one Version) (6.H.1); the `LifecycleStore` modeled on `ApprovalStore` ŌĆö the codebase's only proven across-runs persistence (6.H.2); `CatalogDiff.compose` to close the derivative algebra + flip A-Lifecycle-4 (6.H.3); the change-manifest ŌĆö the manifest integrates ╬┤ (move counts + refactorlog xref + CDC series + tolerance residual + AppliedTransforms *outcome*), not just state (6.H.4). Plus refactorlog-against-prior with real episode time (6.F.1/F6).

**Holding the spine:** persistence is the gap, not algebra (the FTC is proven) ŌĆö reuse `ApprovalStore` as the template, do not invent a parallel store; the verbs reify at the second consumer; each slice closes one named residual of one equation or lights one dark cell of the ┬¦2 field; per-slice rent unchanged.

**Cross-references:** `WAVE_6_MORPHOLOGY.md` (the unification; the four-agent research); `WAVE_6_ALGEBRA.md` ┬¦12 (the concern-movement field; latent/activated; the reification principle); `EXECUTION_PLAN.md` ┬¦6.G + 6.H; `AXIOMS.md` T13/T15 status (latent, sharpened). ## 2026-06-01 ŌĆö Wave 6 prework code: CatalogDiff.compose + norm/channelCounts + Lifecycle.netDiff (6.H.3)

**Resolved (code; the first activation prework, holding the spine).** The most foundational, evidence-justified, spine-respecting prework for both immediate success and the long-term calculus ŌĆö three pure additions in `Projection.Core`, no new dependencies, concrete on `CatalogDiff`/`Lifecycle` (per `WAVE_6_ALGEBRA.md` ┬¦12.4 ŌĆö the schema-side carriers, NOT a generic `Torsor`/`Delta`/`RowDiff`).

- **`CatalogDiff.compose` (the torsor `+`; T13 / A-Lifecycle-4).** `compose d1 d2 : CatalogDiff option` = the net displacement `source d1 ŌåÆ target d2`, `Some` iff d1's target meets d2's source on the captured surface (the groupoid composition is *partial* ŌĆö a non-adjacent pair is `None`, fail-loud). Implemented as `between (source d1) (target d2)` ŌĆö provably the net delta (the functor law `applyDiff (compose d1 d2) (source d1) = applyDiff d2 (applyDiff d1 (source d1))`); associativity follows (both groupings recompute `between(source d1, target dŌéÖ)`). **A-Lifecycle-4 promoted SkipŌåÆFact** (Bucket C ŌåÆ A).
- **`CatalogDiff.norm` + `ChannelCounts` + `channelCounts` (the concrete schema-side `ŌĆ¢┬ĘŌĆ¢` / `ŽĆ`; T14/T15).** The per-channel move counts (renamed/added/removed kinds + added/removed/renamed/changed attributes) and their sum. `norm (between A A) = 0`; `norm d = 0 Ō¤║ isEmpty d` (because `between` stores only non-empty attribute diffs); `norm = ╬Ż channelCounts` (additivity, T14/T15). The foundation the change-manifest (6.H.4) and the `migrate` preview (6.D.1) consume.
- **`Lifecycle.netDiff` (the integral Ōł½╬┤ as a single delta; T13's FTC companion).** `= fold compose` over `evolutionChain` (composability holds by the monotone chain), collapsing to `between genesis latest`; `applyDiff (netDiff lc) genesis Ōēł latest`.

**Witnesses (all live):** `compose: applyDiff (compose d1 d2) A = applyDiff d2 (applyDiff d1 A) (functor law)`; `compose: a non-adjacent pair is None (fail-loud, partial groupoid)`; `compose: associativity ŌĆö (d1+d2)+d3 ŌĆ”` (the A-Lifecycle-4 witness); `norm: between A A has norm 0`; `norm: a non-empty diff has norm > 0`; `norm: equals the sum of the channel counts (additivity, T14/T15)`; `6.H.3: netDiff applied to genesis reproduces latest`; `6.H.3: netDiff equals fold compose over the evolution chain (3 snapshots)`. AxiomTests: A-Lifecycle-4 flipped SkipŌåÆFact; T13 + T15 citations extended.

**Spine held (the deliberate non-builds).** Did NOT build: a generic `Torsor`/abstract `Delta`/`RowDiff` value (the verbs stay concrete on `CatalogDiff` per ┬¦12.4; the data ╬┤ stays substrate-fused); the multi-plane `Episode`/`LifecycleStore` (blocked on a round-trippable `CatalogŌåöJSON` codec that does not exist ŌĆö that is a major slice, 6.H.1ŌĆō6.H.2's prerequisite, not prework, and must not be scaffolded speculatively); the refactorlog-against-prior (gated on decision-owed #1). Each is named-with-trigger.

**Status sharpened (docs kept honest):** `WAVE_6_ALGEBRA.md` ┬¦9 + ┬¦12.2 (T13 compose+netDiff shipped, remaining = durable episode; T14/T15 schema-side norm carrier shipped, data norm Ō¼Ü); `AXIOMS.md` T13/T15 status; `EXECUTION_PLAN.md` 6.H.3 ŌåÆ SHIPPED; `WAVE_6_MORPHOLOGY.md` ┬¦0 prework-landed note.

**Verification.** Pure pool green (2555 passed / 208 skipped / 0 failed); matrix gate PASS, L2 live 79ŌåÆ80 (A-Lifecycle-4), C 8ŌåÆ7, round-trip 5/5.

**Cross-references:** `WAVE_6_ALGEBRA.md` ┬¦9/┬¦12 (the calculus this activates); `EXECUTION_PLAN.md` 6.H.3; `src/Projection.Core/{CatalogDiff,Lifecycle}.fs`; `tests/Projection.Tests/{CatalogDiffTests,LifecycleTests,AxiomTests}.fs`. The remaining 6.H prework prerequisite is the `CatalogŌåöJSON` round-trip codec (gates `LifecycleStore`).

## 2026-06-01 ŌĆö Wave 6: the CatalogŌåöJSON codec ŌĆö the persistence-boundary adjunction (6.H.1/6.H.2 prerequisite)

**Resolved (code).** `CatalogCodec` (`src/Projection.Targets.Json/CatalogCodec.fs`) ŌĆö `serialize : Catalog -> string` and `deserialize : string -> Result<Catalog>`, the durability instance of the `realize`/`ingest` pair whose round-trip law `deserialize (serialize c) = Ok c` is the adjunction applied to disk. This is the gating prerequisite the 6.H.3 entry named: the durable `Episode`/`LifecycleStore` could not be scaffolded until a *total*, *deterministic*, *re-validating* catalog codec existed. It now does.

- **Total (the totality contract is the IR inventory).** Every record field and every DU variant reachable from `Catalog` is encoded AND decoded ŌĆö verified, not asserted: an independent inventory audit walked all 16 records + 17 DUs (+ `SsKey` via its existing `serialize`/`deserialize`) and confirmed zero written-but-not-read, read-but-not-written, or silently-defaulted fields. The hazard the audit specifically cleared: records rebuilt via `{ X.create ŌĆ” with ŌĆ” }` must list every field the `create` call does not set, else decode substitutes a constructor default instead of the round-tripped value (e.g. `Index.AllowRowLocks` defaults `true` in `create` ŌĆö a round-tripped `false` is preserved only because the `with` block names it). All `with` blocks are complete.
- **Deterministic (T1).** `JsonOptions.indented`, fixed write order, `decimal` via InvariantCulture string, `Map<Name,string>` (StaticRow values) emitted in key-sorted order. `serialize` is byte-stable across calls and across a decode round-trip.
- **Re-validating (A39).** Decode rebuilds raw leaf records structurally, then funnels modules/sequences through `Module.create` / `Catalog.create`, so a decoded catalog re-proves the aggregate invariants (disjoint keys, no dangling FK) and surfaces `Result<Catalog>` ŌĆö a corrupt document is a structured `Error`, never an unchecked value or an exception (malformed JSON ŌåÆ `codec.parse` failure). It reuses `SsKey.serialize`/`deserialize` (the identity codec) and the FsToolkit `result {}` CE for decode composition.

**Witnesses (24 tests, all live; `CatalogCodecTests.fs`).** A comprehensive worked-example catalog (every record field off-default; both `DataSpace` shapes; all `DataCompression`/`TemporalRetention`/`ModalityMark` variants; references with `OnDelete`/`OnUpdate`); per-variant enumeration sweeping all 9 `PrimitiveType`, all 34 `SqlStorageType` forms (incl. `SqlLength` Bounded/Max), all 9 `SqlLiteral`, all 7 `ModalityMark`, all 4 `TemporalRetentionUnit`, all `Origin`/`ReferenceAction`/`IndexColumnDirection`/`DataCompressionLevel`/`DataSpace`/`SequenceCacheMode`, all 4 `SsKey` variants, `TableId` ┬▒catalog; and the **universally-quantified round-trip law** `ŌłĆ c. deserialize (serialize c) = Ok c` (+ byte-determinism) via FsCheck over a *constructed-valid* `Catalog` generator (FK targets and index columns drawn from already-chosen keys ŌĆö "validity is constructed, not validated"; the generator reuses the per-variant alphabet lists as its `Gen.elements` sources, so the variant space is enumerated once).

**Test discipline (operator feedback, mid-slice).** Hand-authored JSON in the failure-path tests was flagged as a code smell ŌĆö it duplicates the codec's wire format (field names; the `SsKey` `O32:ŌĆ”` encoding) in a second place, so a codec rename makes the test *lie* rather than *break*. Retired in favor of **declarative DOM edits of the codec's own valid output** (`editing : (JsonNode -> unit) -> Catalog -> string` with pipeable null-safe `field`/`item` navigators): "missing required field" = a valid catalog minus its module `name`; "dangling FK target" = a valid two-kind catalog minus the FK's target kind; "unknown DU tag" = a valid modality mark retagged. The codec stays the single source of wire-format truth. This is the **text-builder-as-first-instinct** discipline (typed structure over raw strings) applied to test inputs, and the property-based law is the most declarative possible statement of the codec's contract.

**Spine held.** No `LifecycleStore`/`Episode` built yet (this is their prerequisite, now unblocked ŌĆö they land named-with-trigger at 6.H.1/6.H.2). Home is `Projection.Targets.Json` (depends on Core only; `Pipeline` already references it ŌĆö no project-ref change needed for a future store). FsToolkit resolves transitively (no new package ref).

**Verification.** Pure pool green (2579 passed / 208 skipped / 0 failed; +24 from the codec); matrix gate PASS, L2 live 80, round-trip 5/5.

**Cross-references:** `EXECUTION_PLAN.md` 6.H.1/6.H.2 (the store this unblocks); `WAVE_6_ALGEBRA.md` ┬¦12.4 (the persistence-boundary adjunction); `src/Projection.Targets.Json/CatalogCodec.fs`; `tests/Projection.Tests/CatalogCodecTests.fs`.

## 2026-06-01 ŌĆö Operating discipline: totality-contract verification for total functions over the IR (the `{ create ŌĆ” with ŌĆ” }` default-substitution hazard)

**Codified (cross-cutting; the codec is the worked precedent).** Every function claiming to be **total over the IR** ŌĆö a codec, a diff (`CatalogDiff.between`/`applyDiff`), an emitter that must mention every kind (T11), a traversal that must visit every variant ŌĆö carries a **totality contract whose source of truth is the IR inventory itself** (the closed DUs + record field lists reachable from `Catalog`). The discipline: **verify totality against the inventory; do not assert it.** "Every variant is handled" is a claim to be *checked* (enumerate the DUs/records; confirm each is reached) ŌĆö by an independent audit pass for keystone artifacts, by exhaustive `match` (the compiler's closed-DU check) wherever the shape allows. A plausible-looking implementation that silently drops one variant is the L1ŌåÆL2 silent-erasure failure class Wave 6 exists to close (6.A.1/6.A.9); it must not reappear at any new total boundary.

**The named hazard ŌĆö `{ X.create ŌĆ” with ŌĆ” }` default-substitution.** When decode/reconstruction rebuilds an IR aggregate record via record-update over a smart constructor (`{ Attribute.create ssKey name typ with Column = ŌĆ”; IsActive = ŌĆ” }`), **every field the `create` call does not set MUST appear in the `with` block** ŌĆö otherwise the field silently takes the *constructor's default*, not the round-tripped/intended value, and the compiler does **not** flag it (record-update with a missing field is legal; it just inherits). This is invisible precisely where defaults are non-trivial: `Index.AllowRowLocks`/`AllowPageLocks` default `true` in `Index.create`, so a `false` is preserved only because the codec's `with` block names it; `Reference.IsConstraintTrusted` defaults `true`; `Kind.IsActive` defaults `true`. **Detection protocol:** cross-check the `create` signature against the record's full field list; (#fields set by `create`) + (#fields in the `with` block) must equal the total field count. **This is the bare-value smart-constructor pattern's dual cost** ŌĆö the pattern absorbs field *extensions* at one site (CLAUDE.md ┬¦Types), but reconstruction sites must then enumerate the non-default fields explicitly. Carry this to every remaining Wave-6 IR-reconstruction site: the `LifecycleStore` envelope (6.H.2), the change-manifest decode (6.H.4), any `migrate` state load (6.D.1).

**Why it earns its place.** F#'s exhaustiveness check covers `match` on closed DUs but NOT record-update field omission; the totality of a record-reconstructing function is therefore *unchecked by the compiler* and must be checked by discipline (the field-count cross-reference) or by a property test (round-trip over a generator that varies every field off its default ŌĆö see the sibling entry). Worked precedent: the `CatalogCodec` totality audit (`DECISIONS 2026-06-01 ŌĆö the CatalogŌåöJSON codec`) cleared all 16 records + 17 DUs this way.

## 2026-06-01 ŌĆö Operating discipline: declarative test inputs + the universal law over a constructed-valid generator

**Codified (cross-cutting; extends text-builder-as-first-instinct to test *inputs*).** Two paired disciplines for testing total/round-trip functions, surfaced by operator feedback during the codec slice ("hand-built JSON is a code smell"):

1. **Test inputs are declarative edits of the producer's own valid output ŌĆö never hand-authored wire format.** Hand-writing a serialized form (JSON, SQL, any wire string) in a test **duplicates the producer's format in a second place**, so a producer rename makes the test *lie* (still green, no longer testing the real shape) rather than *break*. Instead, generate a valid artifact from the producer, then express each failure/edge case as a **structural edit** of it: parse to the typed DOM (`JsonNode`), mutate (remove a required field; retag a DU; delete an FK's target kind), re-serialize. The producer stays the single source of wire-format truth. This is the **text-builder-as-first-instinct** discipline (typed structure over raw strings; CLAUDE.md operating-disciplines table) applied to the *input* side of a test, not just the emission side. Worked precedent: `CatalogCodecTests.editing : (JsonNode -> unit) -> Catalog -> string` with pipeable null-safe `field`/`item` navigators.

2. **State the universal law via a property over a constructed-valid generator; enumerate the variant space once.** Worked examples witness totality *per variant* but do not state the universal contract. The most declarative statement of a round-trip/total-function's correctness is the **universally-quantified law** (`ŌłĆ c. deserialize (serialize c) = Ok c`) as an FsCheck property. For an aggregate with referential invariants (A39), **construct validity, don't generate-and-filter**: draw FK targets and index columns from already-chosen keys so the generator emits only valid values by construction (the codebase's "determinism/validity is constructed, not validated" posture). **Reuse the per-variant alphabet lists** (the `allStorageTypes`/`allLiterals`/`allModalityMarks` lists the enumerated example tests already define) as the generator's `Gen.elements` sources, so the variant space is enumerated **exactly once** and the example-suite and the property-suite cannot drift apart. The two suites are complementary and both belong: the property proves universality + random nesting/combinations; the examples pinpoint *which* variant regressed. Worked precedent: `CatalogCodecTests` (22 worked examples + 2 properties over `catalogGen`).

**FsCheck note (pitfall avoided).** Order `open Xunit` *before* `open FsCheck` ŌĆö with `open FsCheck` in scope, a later `open Xunit` resolves to the nested `FsCheck.Xunit` (FS0893, partial-path-as-error under `TreatWarningsAsErrors`). And `JsonNode`/its indexer are `ŌĆ” | null` under `Nullable=enable`; wrap navigation in a `req : JsonNode | null -> JsonNode` helper rather than chaining the nullable indexer.

**Cross-references:** `tests/Projection.Tests/CatalogCodecTests.fs`; the sibling totality-contract entry above; CLAUDE.md operating-disciplines (text-builder-as-first-instinct; "determinism is constructed, not validated").

## 2026-06-01 ŌĆö Wave 6: the durable provenance substrate ŌĆö Episode + EpisodicLifecycle + LifecycleStore + ChangeManifest (6.H.1/6.H.2/6.H.4)

**Resolved (code; the calculus now integrates over a durable, multi-plane chain).** The morphology's load-bearing gap ŌĆö "there is **no durable episode** to integrate over; the FTC runs only over in-memory test values" ŌĆö is closed. Four shipments, building on the just-landed codec:

- **`Episode` + `EpisodicLifecycle` (6.H.1; `Projection.Core/Episode.fs`).** An `Episode` co-records the five concerns at one `EpisodeCoordinate` (`Version ├Ś Environment ├Ś At`): Schema (`Catalog`), Data (`DataObservation` = CDC capture count + handle), Identity (inside the `Catalog`'s `SsKey`s), Time (the coordinate), Decision (the emitted `RefactorLogRef`). The `Profile` is co-recorded **in-memory** (the data-plane evidence) but is not durable (┬¦12.4). `EpisodicLifecycle` is the monotone chain (append enforces L3-L2 on the `Version` ordinal); its schema-plane FTC ŌĆö `reconstructLatestSchema` / `netSchemaDiff` / `schemaEvolutionChain` ŌĆö is the **same `CatalogDiff` algebra `Lifecycle` runs, projected onto `Episode.Schema`** (parallel reimplementation, not a shared generic: the two-consumer threshold doesn't justify abstracting over "thing with a `.Catalog` and `.Version`", and the cross-type-error friction of projecting through `Lifecycle` would be worse).
- **`LifecycleStore` (6.H.2; `Projection.Pipeline/LifecycleStore.fs`).** Durable JSON persistence modeled on `ApprovalStore.fs` (deterministic `Utf8JsonWriter`, T1-stable order, fail-closed structured errors), **composing `CatalogCodec`** for each episode's schema plane (`jw.WriteRawValue` on save; `schemaEl.GetRawText() |> CatalogCodec.deserialize` on load ŌĆö reuse the codec, never re-encode the `Catalog`). The FTC now reconstructs over a chain **loaded from disk**. `Profile` is not persisted ŌåÆ a loaded episode equals its `Episode.durableProjection`. A missing/malformed file is a fail-closed `ParseFailure` (no empty-lifecycle default ŌĆö a lifecycle always opens at genesis, unlike `ApprovalRegistry`); codec re-validation (A39) reaches through the boundary.
- **`ChangeManifest` (6.H.4 Core algebra; `Projection.Core/ChangeManifest.fs`).** The change-manifest **of ╬┤** ŌĆö the per-edge *displacement* (`ChannelCounts` + `SchemaNorm` ŌĆ¢╬┤ŌĆ¢ + refactorlog xref + CDC capture count), not the target state. `between` computes it from two episodes; `series` is the per-edge list over a chain; `pathLength` (╬Ż edge norms) vs `netSchemaDiff |> norm` (net displacement) exposes a timeline's **churn** (a rename-then-rename-back has path-length 2, net 0).

**Environment naming corrected to the operator's reality (ubiquitous language, pillar 8).** The active cutover rotation is **Dev ŌåÆ Qa ŌåÆ Uat** (`Prod` carried for forward-readiness, **not yet in rotation**). The canonical `Transfer.Environment` DU's prior `Test` variant was the placeholder for what the operator calls **QA** ŌĆö renamed `Test`ŌåÆ`Qa` (DU variant + `Environment.name` "TEST"ŌåÆ"QA" + the CLI `"QA"` parse). Tiny blast radius (Transfer.fs + Program.fs); V2-no-back-compat, so no `"TEST"` alias retained. The `Episode` reuses this canonical `Environment` rather than minting its own (one Environment concept across Transfer + Episode).

**Witnesses (23 tests, all live):** `EpisodeTests` (co-recording acceptance; monotone append; `durableProjection`; schema-FTC `reconstructLatestSchema`/`netSchemaDiff`/`schemaEvolutionChain`); `ChangeManifestTests` (displacement record; idempotent-edge norm 0; series-per-edge; churn = path-length ŌłÆ net); `LifecycleStoreTests` (exact durable round-trip; FTC over the persisted chain; byte-determinism; `Named`-env round-trip; Profile-drop = `durableProjection`; three fail-closed paths incl. codec re-validation through the boundary). AxiomTests: T13 extended to cite the durable FTC; new 6.H.1/6.H.2 + 6.H.4 entries.

**Spine held (the deliberate non-builds).** Did NOT build: a generic `Torsor`/abstract `Delta`/`RowDiff` (the verbs stay concrete on `CatalogDiff`, ┬¦12.4); a `Profile` codec (the data ╬┤'s durable form is the CDC series, not a serialized Profile ŌĆö persisting it is the speculative-value trap); the `SsdtManifest` *emission* wiring of the change section (6.H.4 deferred leg ŌĆö lands when a manifest consumer demands the on-disk form); the `migrate` orchestrator (6.D.1) that writes runs into the substrate. Each is named-with-trigger.

**Disciplines applied (from the codec slice).** Totality-contract verification (the `{ X.create ŌĆ” with ŌĆ” }` hazard) on `Episode.durableProjection` and the store's reconstruction; the store's failure tests follow the declarative-input discipline (corrupt the producer's *own* output ŌĆö rename the codec-only `"physical"` property ŌĆö rather than hand-author wire format).

**Verification.** Pure pool green (2602 passed / 208 skipped / 0 failed; +23); matrix gate PASS, L2 live 80, round-trip 5/5.

**Cross-references:** `EXECUTION_PLAN.md` 6.H.1/6.H.2/6.H.4 (all LANDED); `WAVE_6_ALGEBRA.md` ┬¦9 (T13 activated) + ┬¦12.4; `WAVE_6_MORPHOLOGY.md` ┬¦0 (the "no durable episode" finding RESOLVED); `src/Projection.Core/{Episode,ChangeManifest}.fs`; `src/Projection.Pipeline/LifecycleStore.fs`. The remaining 6.H reach is the `migrate` orchestrator (6.D.1) that records runs into this substrate.

## 2026-06-01 ŌĆö Wave 6: `migrate A B` ŌĆö the L3 composition (Promise 8; T16 activated)

**Resolved (code; the bullseye's structural composition + durable loop).** The one-command `migrate A B` ŌĆö the apex's culminating operation (NORTH_STAR Promise 8; the L3 bullseye) ŌĆö exists as a composition and round-trips. The morphology's two darkest findings ("the diff-machinery has **zero production callers**"; "the engine ships `realize(B)`, not `emit(B ŌŖ¢ A)` ŌĆö the differential leg of T16 is dark") are closed at the composition level.

- **`Migration` (Core; `Projection.Core/Migration.fs`).** The pure plan: `Migration.plan allowDrops A B = emit(B ŌŖ¢ A)` ŌĆö observe the displacement (`CatalogDiff.between`), produce the `MigrationPreview` (the change-manifest of ╬┤ **at plan time**: channel counts + norm + the rename/add/remove/reshape moves), and detect the fail-loud `MigrationViolation`s (destructive `WouldDropKind`/`WouldDropAttribute`, refused unless `allowDrops` ŌĆö a drop is data loss the displacement cannot undo; a *rename* is never a violation, `ŌĆ¢renameŌĆ¢_data = 0`, A43). `Migration.applyTo (plan A B) A ŌēĪ B` (modulo the captured surface) is **T16, the master equation** ŌĆö the structural round-trip the AŌåÆB canary asserts. `Migration.toEpisode` turns a completed migration into the next durable `Episode` (the 6.H integration).
- **`MigrationRun` (Pipeline; `Projection.Pipeline/MigrationRun.fs`).** The orchestrator: `preview` composes the **minimum-viable** schema differential (`SchemaMigrationEmitter` ŌĆö ALTER, never CREATE) + the data-preserving renames (`RefactorLogEmitter`) from one displacement, refusing **before any write** on (a) drops without `allowDrops` (`RefusedByViolations`), (b) the emitter's non-shape-facet `Error`s (`RefusedBySchemaErrors`). `record` closes the loop: it appends the migration's episode to the `LifecycleStore` (genesis on the first migration of a timeline; fail-closed `NonMonotonic`/`StoreError` otherwise), so the FTC over the recorded chain reproduces B across a disk reload ŌĆö the durable AŌåÆB round-trip.
- **T16 promoted SkipŌåÆFact (Bucket C ŌåÆ A).** Its skip trigger was literally "the one-command `migrate A B` canary"; the structural canary is now green. The migrate composition is the production caller the diff emitters never had.

**The composition IS the algebra (not new emission).** Every stage was an existing capability; `migrate` is their composition under the change calculus ŌĆö `between` (6.A.10, the displacement) ŌåÆ `{RefactorLog, ALTER}` (T14, the channel partition: renames and reshapes never touch the same element) ŌåÆ `Episode`/`LifecycleStore` (6.H, the durable record) ŌåÆ the FTC (`reconstructLatestSchema` reproduces B). `migrate A B = emit(B ŌŖ¢ A)`, not `realize(B)`: a reshape previews one ALTER, a rename previews one RefactorLog entry, an idempotent `migrate A A` previews **empty** (zero touches, CDC-silent).

**Deferred (named-with-trigger ŌĆö the live-execute leg).** The `projection migrate --source-conn <A> --target <B-config> --execute` CLI that reads A from a *live deployed DB* (`ReadSide`), runs the connection/permission pre-flights (6.C.1), and executes the differential against *real substrates* (deploy + RefactorLog-aware transfer, transactional 6.C.2) ŌĆö composes the shipped plan with the existing `Deploy`/`Transfer`, and lands with the **Docker AŌåÆB canary** (the live square's last confirmation). The *structural* square commutes and the durable provenance loop is green now; the *live* SQL execution is the remaining leg. Also not built: the two-catalog-load CLI preview surface (deferred with the execute leg, since the operator's `migrate` reads A from a connection, not a file).

**Witnesses (18 tests, all live).** `MigrationTests` (T16 round-trip over rename/reshape/add targets; idempotent AŌåÆA; minimum-viable touches by channel; fail-loud drop violations ┬▒ `allowDrops`; `toEpisode`). `MigrationRunTests` (the ALTER-not-CREATE composition; renameŌåÆRefactorLog; empty idempotent artifacts; `RefusedByViolations`/`RefusedBySchemaErrors`; the **full AŌåÆB loop** ŌĆö migrate, record, reconstruct reproduces B across a disk reload; `NonMonotonic` refusal). AxiomTests: T16 flipped SkipŌåÆFact citing the master-equation + durable-loop witnesses.

**Verification.** Pure pool green (2623 passed / 207 skipped / 0 failed; +19 passed, ŌłÆ1 skip as T16 flipped); matrix gate PASS, L2 live 83 (T16 promoted), C 6, round-trip 5/5.

**Cross-references:** `EXECUTION_PLAN.md` 6.D.1 (LANDED ŌĆö composition + durable loop; live-execute deferred); `NORTH_STAR.md` Promise 8 + ┬¦1c; `WAVE_6_ALGEBRA.md` ┬¦9 (T16 activated); `WAVE_6_MORPHOLOGY.md` ┬¦0 (the zero-production-callers + dark-T16 findings RESOLVED); `src/Projection.Core/Migration.fs`; `src/Projection.Pipeline/MigrationRun.fs`. The remaining apex reach is the live-execute leg + the Docker AŌåÆB canary.

## 2026-06-01 ŌĆö Wave 6: `migrate A B` runs on SQL Server ŌĆö the live square commutes (6.D.1 live-execute leg)

**Resolved (code; the L3 bullseye executes against real SQL Server).** The composition's live-execute leg ŌĆö the last residual of Promise 8 ŌĆö landed. `migrate A B` is no longer only a structural/durable witness; it **evolves a deployed database** in one command.

- **`MigrationRun.execute allowDrops source target cnn` (Pipeline).** The live executor: refuse fail-loud (drops / non-shape facets) **before any write**; execute the minimum-viable differential in physical order ŌĆö the **rename channel** (`renameStatements`: `sp_rename` of the physical table **plus** an `sp_updateextendedproperty` re-binding `V2.LogicalName` to the new `Name`) then the `ALTER`/`ADD` differential (`SchemaMigrationEmitter`); read **B'** back (`ReadSide.read`); verify B' reproduces B at the **schema-structural** level. Takes the *known* source A explicitly (the prior recorded episode / authored A ŌĆö noise-free, no `ReadSide`-fidelity drift in the diff); `executeFromLive` is the bootstrap variant that reads A from the connection first. Idempotent + resumable **by construction** ŌĆö re-running re-diffs the current state, so a partial run completes on re-run and a fully-migrated DB is a no-op.
- **`PhysicalSchema.isSchemaEqual` (Core) ŌĆö new primitive.** Schema-structural equality (columns + FKs + logical-name bindings + annotations), **ignoring row data** (`*Rows`/`*RowDigests`). The right predicate for a *schema* migration's verification: B' must reproduce B's **structure**; the rows it carries are the **preserved/migrated data** (the whole point of a differential over a drop+recreate), not part of the schema target B (a definition, not a data set). The full `isEqual` stays the predicate for the static-seed round-trip canaries.
- **The canary caught a real fidelity bug.** First run failed: after `sp_rename`, the physical table was `MIGAB_PATRON` but its `V2.LogicalName` extended property still said `Customer` ŌĆö `sp_rename` renames the *object* but not the logical-name anchor (V2's A1 identity binding). The PhysicalSchema diff surfaced it precisely (`logical=Customer` extra / `logical=Patron` missing). Fix: the rename channel **always** re-binds `V2.LogicalName` (even for a logical-only rename where no `sp_rename` fires). This is exactly the canary-as-forcing-function discipline paying out.
- **`projection migrate --from <model-a.json> --to <model-b.json> [--allow-drops]` (CLI).** The operator dry-run surface ŌĆö loads both states (`Compose.read`), prints the minimum-viable plan (the change-manifest of ╬┤: norm + per-channel counts + the renames + statement counts), fail-loud (non-zero exit) on drops without opt-in.

**The Docker AŌåÆB canary (`MigrationCanaryTests`, green).** One `execute` evolves a deployed state-A DB to B across **three channels at once** ŌĆö a physical table rename (`MIGAB_CUSTOMER` ŌåÆ `MIGAB_PATRON`), a widening reshape (`EMAIL` NVARCHAR(50) ŌåÆ 256), and a new column (`LOYALTY`): (1) B' reproduces B (schema-structural, `isSchemaEqual`); (2) the old table is gone, the new exists (a rename, not a drop+recreate); (3) `EMAIL` widened; (4) `LOYALTY` exists; (5) **the seeded row survived** the renamed+reshaped table; (6) the re-run is idempotent (empty differential). A second canary proves `migrate B A` **refuses** the destructive drop before touching the live DB (the row + Loyalty column survive intact).

**Spine held / remaining reach (named-with-trigger).** The `--source-conn`/`--execute` CLI flags (the live execution function `executeFromLive` exists; the flag plumbing + the connection/permission pre-flights 6.C.1 are the wiring); RefactorLog-aware **cross-table data transfer** (the in-place schema evolution already preserves data ŌĆö a cross-table move composes the existing `Transfer`); **column renames** (table renames ship). Each is wiring over the shipped algebra, not new mechanism.

**Witnesses.** `MigrationRunTests` (+2 pure: `renameStatements` for a physical rename = `sp_rename` + logical re-bind; a logical-only rename = re-bind without `sp_rename`). `MigrationCanaryTests` (the 3-channel live AŌåÆB + the live drop-refusal). AxiomTests: T16 extended to cite the live canary.

**Verification.** Pure pool green (2625 passed / 207 skipped / 0 failed; +2); Docker AŌåÆB canary green (3/3); matrix gate PASS, L2 live 83, round-trip 5/5.

**Cross-references:** `EXECUTION_PLAN.md` 6.D.1 (LANDED ŌĆö composition + durable loop + live execution); `NORTH_STAR.md` Promise 8 + ┬¦1c (the live square commutes); `WAVE_6_ALGEBRA.md` ┬¦9 (T16 realized on SQL Server); `WAVE_6_MORPHOLOGY.md` ┬¦0 (the live square commutes ŌĆö the calculus is wired, executed, durable); `src/Projection.Pipeline/MigrationRun.fs`; `src/Projection.Core/PhysicalSchema.fs` (`isSchemaEqual`); `tests/Projection.Tests/MigrationCanaryTests.fs`. The remaining apex reach is the `--execute` CLI wiring + cross-table data transfer.

## 2026-06-01 ŌĆö Wave 6: migrate column renames + cross-substrate data transfer (6.D.1 extension)

**Resolved (code; the two named-with-trigger reaches the live-execute leg deferred).** `migrate A B` now moves **column renames** and **data** ŌĆö both live on SQL Server.

- **Column renames (the second rename channel).** `MigrationRun.renameStatements` now emits, in addition to table renames, the **column** rename channel from `AttributeDiff.Renamed` (already detected by `between`): per renamed attribute, `sp_rename '<schema>.<table>.<oldCol>', '<newCol>', 'COLUMN'` (only if the physical `ColumnName` changed) + a **column-level** `V2.LogicalName` re-bind (`@level2type=N'COLUMN'`). Ordering is **table renames ŌåÆ column renames ŌåÆ ALTERs**, so every later statement references the post-rename physical names. The column-level logical re-bind mirrors the table-level fix (the canary proved `sp_rename` leaves the logical anchor stale at every level). Live canary: a column rename (`EMAIL` ŌåÆ `CONTACT`) executes via `sp_rename ŌĆ” 'COLUMN'` and **preserves data**.
- **Cross-substrate data transfer (the DevŌåÆUAT composition).** `MigrationRun.executeWithData allowDrops mode allowCdc sinkSource target reconciliation dataSource sink` evolves the **sink**'s schema in place to B (the minimum-viable + fail-loud `execute`), then ŌĆö **only if the schema leg verified** (never load into an unverified target) ŌĆö transfers rows from the data `source` into the sink over the agreed contract B via the existing `Transfer` engine (`Transfer.run`, or `Transfer.runReconciling` for the User re-key). New `MigrationDataOutcome { Schema; Transfer }` carries both reports; new `MigrationError.DataTransferFailed`. This is the premise's "migrate Dev data up to UAT by producing a full-export whose users have been rekeyed" ŌĆö schema + data composed into one operation. Live canary: `executeWithData` migrates a sink AŌåÆB and loads the source's rows into the migrated table.

**Layering note.** `MigrationRun.fs` moved after `TransferRun.fs` in the Pipeline compile order (it now composes `Transfer`). The schema executor (`execute`/`renameStatements`) is unchanged; `executeWithData` is the new cross-substrate leg.

**Witnesses.** `MigrationRunTests` (+1 pure: a column rename emits `sp_rename ŌĆ” 'COLUMN'` + the column-level logical re-bind). `MigrationCanaryTests` (+2 live: the column-rename canary; the `executeWithData` schema+data canary ŌĆö both green on real SQL Server). AxiomTests: T16 extended to cite the `executeWithData` live witness.

**Remaining reach (named-with-trigger).** The `--source-conn`/`--sink-conn`/`--execute` CLI flags wiring `executeFromLive` / `executeWithData` against live connections ŌĆö the execution functions exist; the flag plumbing + the connection/permission pre-flights (6.C.1) are the wiring.

**Verification.** Pure pool green (2626 passed / 207 skipped / 0 failed; +1); Docker migration canaries green (5/5 ŌĆö AŌåÆB 3-channel, drop-refusal, column rename, data transfer); matrix gate PASS, L2 live 83, round-trip 5/5.

**Cross-references:** `EXECUTION_PLAN.md` 6.D.1 (column renames + data transfer shipped); `WAVE_6_ALGEBRA.md` ┬¦9 (T16 ŌĆö schema *and* data live); `WAVE_6_MORPHOLOGY.md` ┬¦0 (migrate moves schema and data); `src/Projection.Pipeline/MigrationRun.fs`; `tests/Projection.Tests/MigrationCanaryTests.fs`. The remaining apex reach is the live-connection CLI flag wiring.

## 2026-06-02 ŌĆö Wave 6.A: Decision/Schema faithfulness ŌĆö surrogate-capture refusals, un-hollowed ReadSide (indexes + FK-trust), the A42 FK-readback fix, and the named-record lint

**Resolved (code).** Five silent erasures on the Decision/Schema axes turned into held theorems, plus the pre-existing A42 red fixed and a new lint rule for the operator's "named maps and lists" discipline.

- **6.A.2 / 6.A.3 ŌĆö Transfer Execute-time surrogate-capture refusals (`TransferRun.executeGate`).** Two `AssignedBySink` shapes the per-row capture path cannot honor are now refused, fail-loud, before any write (mirroring the CDC pre-flight): a **cyclic** `AssignedBySink` load (an IDENTITY-PK kind with a cycle-deferred FK ŌĆö the Phase-2 re-point would key on the source PK the Sink replaced, matching zero rows ŌåÆ `transfer.cyclicAssignedBySink`), and a **composite-identity** `AssignedBySink` kind (multi-column PK ŌĆö single-column capture would truncate the surrogate ŌåÆ `transfer.compositeSurrogateUnsupported`). Both are pure decisions (`cyclicAssignedBySinkKinds` / `compositeAssignedBySinkKinds`) folded with the existing unsatisfiable-cycle check into one `executeGate`, so the fast-pool unit test and the data canary witness the SAME refusal (the 6.A.1 pattern). The correct re-point (cyclic) and tuple-key capture (composite) are the named follow-ons.
- **6.A.5 ŌĆö un-hollow `ReadSide` (indexes + FK-trust).** `ReadSide` no longer hardcodes `Kind.Indexes = []`: a new `readIndexes` batch (`sys.indexes Ōŗł sys.index_columns`, excluding PK-backing / heaps / INCLUDE columns) + `attachIndexes` reconstruct non-PK indexes (incl. `IsUnique`), resolving key columns to the kind's `Attribute.SsKey` by column name. The FK batch now carries `sys.foreign_keys.is_not_trusted`, threaded as `Reference.IsConstraintTrusted = not is_not_trusted` ŌĆö closing the named smart-constructor default-substitution hazard (the `{ Reference.create ŌĆ” with ŌĆ” }` would otherwise silently inherit the `true` default). A deployed UNIQUE index + an enabled-but-untrusted (`WITH NOCHECK`) FK now survive deploy ŌåÆ ReadSide.
- **A42 fixed (the pre-existing 2.4-canary red).** Root cause: `buildKind` recovers a kind's **persisted `V2.SsKey`**, but `buildReference` synthesized the FK **target** kind key via `kindSsKey` (`READSIDE_KIND`) ŌĆö so on the emitŌåÆdeployŌåÆreadback path the FK's `TargetKind` never matched the persisted-SsKey parent, and `PhysicalSchema.ForeignKeys` (`Map.tryFind r.TargetKind kindByKey`) dropped it (0 FKs).
