## 2026-06-05 ŌĆö The dynamic display: progressive disclosure (not a Voice register) serves the expert; every word is Apple-clear

**Decided (operator-led, dynamic-display build; reframes `INSTRUMENT_BACKLOG` slice 4).** Two corrections, both operator audibles, that bind how the display speaks and how it reveals depth.

**1. Progressive disclosure, not a Voice register, is the mechanism.** The slice-4 "Voice layer (plain Ōåö expert *words*)" was a mis-derivation. THE_INSTRUMENT's thesis ŌĆö *"the newcomer is the power user ŌĆ” Power is not a denser interface ŌĆö it is fluency at the dig"* ŌĆö is served by **velocity through depth**, never by swapping plain English for terse notation. The **expert is not the agent**; they are a human operator who carries the Phase-6 ontology and deserves the *same* calm, Apple-style ease as the newcomer. A jargon "expert mode" (e.g. `ŌĆ¢╬┤ŌĆ¢ = 14 ┬Ę gate=ŌĆ” ┬Ę exit 9` in place of the verdict) treats them as a machine parser and is **rejected** ŌĆö there is no expert mode. The instrument presents the high-level topology and lets anyone **zoom into complexity on demand, one level per step, never the deep end.** The `Voice` type + `--voice` flag are dropped; the `Surface` abstraction (essence + dig) is kept as the seed of a depth-bearing node.

**The substrate (built this session): `View.Disclosure`.** A recursive `Disclosure of headline: string * Status * detail: View list` (Apple-HIG disclosure control), depth-aware in the renderer: the headline always shows; `View.writeToDepth depth` reveals `detail` to `depth` levels and collapses deeper nodes behind a `Ō¢Ė N more` affordance (`Lane` is depth-aware likewise; `Ō¢Ė`/`Ō¢Š` are glyph-paired Theme tokens). The calm default is **one level open** (operator-chosen). **Discriminating predicate:** `toJson` carries the full `detail` + `count` **regardless of render depth** ŌĆö the machine lens never loses the tree the human collapsed (a naive "json mirrors the screen" drops the collapsed detail and fails). `--depth N|all` on `diff`; `--format json` is always the full tree. Two consumers: the changeset (move-lanes) + the gate (the formal proof under a `Details` disclosure). The Explore navigator (slice 6) is then "vary the depth budget along a cursor" ŌĆö no new model. *Open follow-up (the glance, slice 5):* at `--depth 0` the move-lanes collapse but the `ŌĆ¢╬┤ŌĆ¢` panel still renders fully; fold it behind a disclosure when the topological glance lands.

**2. Apple-clear diction is a discipline** (`DYNAMIC_DISPLAY.md` ┬¦7.8). Every operator-facing word ŌĆö verdict, affordance, action, disclosure headline ŌĆö is written to the gold standard of **a content team that dwelled on the exact right words: targeted, concrete, simple, calm.** Banned: opaque / mystical wording (the operator's worked counterexamples ŌĆö *"needs your nod"*, *"open a lane"*), drama (*"destroys structure"*), negation-framing (*"nothing destroyed"*), and **leaked internal identifiers** (a human reads `Country`, never `OS_KIND_Country`). The formal proof / notation (`ŌĆ¢╬┤ŌĆ¢`, exit codes, gate labels) is **the dig** ŌĆö the essence made rigorous, reachable by anyone ŌĆö never the essence itself.

**Cross-references:** `DYNAMIC_DISPLAY.md` ┬¦4 (the VoiceŌåÆdisclosure + diction forks) + ┬¦7 discipline 8; `src/Projection.Cli/View.fs` (`Disclosure`, `writeToDepth`, `defaultDepth`); `src/Projection.Cli/Surface.fs` (the kept essence+dig seed); `src/Projection.Cli/Theme.fs` (`collapsed`/`expanded`); `tests/Projection.Tests/ViewTests.fs` (the render-depth vs json-fidelity predicate); `INSTRUMENT_BACKLOG.md` ┬¦4 row 4.

---

**`DECISIONS 2026-06-06` ŌĆö The operator-facing voice: a twelve-rule register (evidential literalism) + the Event/Aggregate/Voice separation. (Amends the 2026-06 "Apple-clear diction is a discipline" entry.)**

Resolved across an operator-led session on the dynamic-display / voice branch. Three anchor docs now carry it: `THE_VOICE.md` (the register), `THE_STORYBOARD.md` (the surface, scene by scene), `THE_VOICE_INTEGRATION.md` (the build plan). The build has not started; this records what is settled before handoff.

**1. The register ŌĆö authoritative, scientific, mature, humble; under evidential literalism.** The prior "Apple-clear diction" framing was *conversational* ŌĆö possessive ("your data stayed put"), first-person ("I checked it matches"), warm ("It's yours"). That register is **retired**. The voice is now a precise measurement report a newcomer can read aloud, governed by one principle ŌĆö **evidential literalism**: every sentence asserts a proposition grounded in its evidence, about a concretely-named subject, ordered by its true structure, with no rhetorical figure standing in for that precision. Operationalized as **twelve rules** (`THE_VOICE.md` ┬¦1): (1) no pronouns; (2) direction by imperative; (3) legible statement with the formal substantiation one level beneath; (4) verdicts asserted (the engine states what it proves; hedge only on genuine interpretation, e.g. a config recommendation); (5) the true verb (Drops/Deletes/Narrows ŌĆö no euphemism, no drama); (6) gentle and direct, never colloquial; (7) neutral reference to the estate (never "your"); (8) ground every claim in its evidence (no "X, not Y" antithesis); (9) order by real structure (perfect aspect + explicit consequence); (10) name the exact referent; (11) concrete definite subjects; (12) stative, agentless voice.

**2. Rule 12 (stative, agentless voice) is the load-bearing refinement.** Removing the pronoun was insufficient: "Read the model ŌĆö 300 tables" still narrates an implied actor. The pipeline reports *states and events*, never *actions it performed* ŌĆö subject-led, passive or resultative, nominalizing a completed process ("Model read complete ŌĆö 300 tables"). **Exception:** a gerund naming a live activity in progress is a state, not a deed ("Reading the model" is kept). Consequence: "self-check" was renamed **"round-trip verification"** (the "self" reintroduced the subject the register removes).

**3. The Event / Aggregate / Voice separation (the discipline; = integration-plan slice 0).** A site emits a *coded event* (a Code + typed payload, no prose) AND **declares its copy as a separable, harvested value** ŌĆö the `TransformRegistry` pattern: declared at the site, harvested by a `Voice.all`, consumed by the renderer, held honest by a `code Ōćö copy` totality test. Inline prose welded to control flow (`sprintf` mid-fold) is **forbidden**. Voice is concern-shaped (a sibling of Lineage / Diagnostics / Bench / Registry) but has **no runtime write side** ŌĆö the declarations are static copy data, projected at render.
- **Core-purity recommendation (reversible).** The event is emitted at the site (pure); the copy declaration lives as close as `F#-pure-core` allows ŌĆö in-module for boundary / pipeline / CLI sites; a 1:1 projection-layer companion for pure-Core passes (`Voice.NullabilityPass Ōåö NullabilityPass`). Typed move/gate `toView`s may sit beside their Core DUs under the `RemovalReason.toDiagnosticString` terminal-projection precedent. *Open: whether the declarations may live literally in the Core pass modules ŌĆö a one-line flip, weighed only against F#-pure-core; settle in slice 1 and record it.*

**4. The catalog shape ŌĆö hybrid.** Typed `toView` projections for the payload-shaped surfaces (the eleven moves, the gates, the proofs); a code-keyed declarative catalog for the flat high-volume codes (lifecycle / errors / config). Each mechanism where it fits.

**5. The live surface ŌĆö streaming Watch from the start; defer the Diagnostics lift.** Per-stage live progress + ETA is in scope for the first build (raises slice-2 scope onto the `LogSink` envelope spine + stage codes + a streaming render path). The Diagnostics lift (a typed `DiagnosticPayload` DU replacing pass prose) is **deferred** behind a real consumer (slice 5), per IR-grows-under-evidence.

**6. "dig" retired from the voice vocabulary** ŌåÆ "the statement" / "the substantiation"; navigation reads "Show detail". The vision thesis ("one essence, infinitely diggable", `THE_INSTRUMENT.md`) is **kept** (operator call). The `Surface.fs` `essence` / `dig` code fields are the matching rename, **pending** the next time `Surface` is touched. `DECISIONS.md`'s prior diction entry is left as-recorded (append-only; its "dig" references describe `Surface.fs` as it was).

**7. The storyboard adds the concern-movement field as the surface's meta-shape** (`WAVE_6_MORPHOLOGY` ┬¦2): the emission axis (this run, integrated by `ChangeManifest`) ├Ś the episode axis (across runs, integrated by `LifecycleStore` / the FTC) ├Ś depth; plus a build-readiness map (LIVE / PARTIAL / LATENT, from the 2026-06-02 debrief) showing the engine already *proves* the migration end to end ŌĆö what is unbuilt is almost entirely voice and surface (the streaming Watch, the record line, the timeline / ladder, the depth layer). The P-6 worked proof (`THE_STORYBOARD.md` ┬¦8) storyboards `migrate` frame by frame as the granularity target.

**Cross-references:** `THE_VOICE.md` (the twelve-rule register, lexicon, banned list); `THE_STORYBOARD.md` (nine acts ┬Ę six streams ┬Ę the concern-movement field ┬Ę the build-readiness map ┬Ę the P-6 worked proof); `THE_VOICE_INTEGRATION.md` (the slice sequence 0ŌĆō5; ┬¦8 locked decisions); `HANDOFF_VOICE_2026_06_06.md` (the executing-agent letter). Amends the 2026-06 "Apple-clear diction is a discipline" entry (its conversational register, "the dig", and "the essence" are superseded). The integration plan's slice 0 (this discipline) is recorded here, so an executing agent starts at slice 1.

---

## 2026-06-07 ŌĆö The CLI is four verbs over one `MovementSpec` (project / check / explain / seal)

**Decision.** The operator surface is re-envisioned from ~16 flat verbs (at
mixed altitudes ŌĆö `skeleton` was a sub-mode sitting beside `transfer`, a whole
protein) to **four intents over one engine**: `project` (all data movement),
`check` (fidelity ŌĆö canary / drift / data / ready), `explain` (understand ŌĆö
diff / policy / node / suggest / migrate-preview), `seal` (provenance ŌĆö eject /
approve). The emission-family verbs collapse into one typed `MovementSpec`
(`Destination ├Ś ModelSource ├Ś Scope ├Ś Strategy ├Ś DataOrigin ├Ś Baseline ├Ś Shape ├Ś
Rekey ├Ś Reconcile ├Ś ŌĆ”`); `deploy` / `migrate` / `load` / `export` are one act ŌĆö
`emit(B ŌŖ¢ A)` ŌĆö distinguished only by the destination and the **auto-read
baseline A** (the engine reads the live state itself, so the operator never picks
deploy-vs-migrate; idempotent redeploy falls out as `ŌĆ¢B ŌŖ¢ AŌĆ¢ = 0`).

**Why.** The verb sprawl was the morphology's "latent calculus" symptom ŌĆö because
the delta-functor was never the *organizing principle of the surface*, each
protein grew its own verb with its own conditional tree. The interface question
is a **namespace of outcomes over the same input** (the protein catalog IS the
namespace), not the engine's algebra exposed; the orthogonality matrix is a red
herring at the interface (a namespace names only the wanted points). See
`THE_CLI.md` (the target design) + `THE_CLI_BACKLOG.md` (the shipped slices).

**Shape.** `Surface.parse` (Pipeline) turns argv into a typed `Intent`; the CLI
executors **delegate to the existing proven `run*` engine faces** (`Compose` /
`Deploy` / `MigrationRun` / `TransferRun` / `DriftRun` / `EjectRun` /
`DataIntegrityChecker` / `PolicyDiff` / `FullExportRun`), so exit codes and
behavior are preserved *by construction*. Targets + the default model are named
in `projection.json` (or `PROJECTION_CONFIG`); a target's `conn` is a `env:` /
`file:` **reference**, never a literal string (D9 holds in config). Config holds
addressing + benign defaults only; intent/danger (`--go`, `--allow-drops`,
`--rekey`) stay on the command line. The legacy 16-verb dispatch + the Argu DUs
(`FullExportArgs` / `TransferArgs` / `VerifyDataArgs` / `VerbArgs`) + the Argu
package are **removed** (deprecate, don't shim).

**Honored vs noted.** `--scope data` routes to the DML-only transfer; `--scope
schema` skips the data leg; `--reconcile` threads to the re-key. `--how` /
`--from` / `--data synthetic|none` are **accepted but surfaced as a named note**
(`noteUnhonored`) ŌĆö the current engine emits its default; wiring those knobs is
an evidence-gated follow-up (no silent drop). Consciously dropped (named):
`full-export --mute-category` / `--debug`; the `emit --config` `[accepted]`
console narration (the data rides the NDJSON stream).

**Witness.** `MovementSurfaceTests` (21 pure tests); pure pool 2829 / 0;
runtime smoke (help, routing, aliasing, D9 refusal, exit codes). Surface types:
`src/Projection.Pipeline/MovementSpec.fs` + `MovementSurface.fs`; executors:
`src/Projection.Cli/Program.fs`.

---

## 2026-06-07 ŌĆö The perf gate runs warm-only; auto-warming from the docker-probe is rejected (zombie risk)

**Decision.** `scripts/perf-gate.sh` gains a **warm-only precondition**: it gates
only when a warm SQL Server is reachable (the `projection-mssql-warm` container
is running, or `PROJECTION_MSSQL_CONN_STR` is set); otherwise it soft-skips
(exit 0). The baseline is recorded warm; a cold-start run (ephemeral container +
~2GB image pull) inflates every timing label 2-10├Ś and **false-trips the gate
while the canary stays GREEN** ŌĆö the recurring "REGRESSION in N labels" the
operator kept seeing.

**Root cause (diagnosed).** `session-start.sh` warms the container only if Docker
is ready inside its 20s window; when Docker comes up later (the `docker-probe.sh`
auto-repair brings up only the *daemon*), the warm container is never
established, so the Stop-hook gate ran the canary cold. The hard gate's trippers
were borderline CPU-time labels (Ōēź50ms) under cold-start + 4-core contention ŌĆö
**no count/volume label ever regressed** (those are deterministic), confirming
it was never a code regression.

**Rejected alternative ŌĆö lazy warm-kick from `docker-probe.sh`.** An attempt to
have the probe kick `warm-sql.sh start` in the background once Docker is up was
**reverted**: on the no-swap host the capped SQL container still OOM-died
(`Exited 255`), leaving a **zombie warm container** that poisons the
warm-honoring test runs (`test.sh docker/canary/focus` + `EphemeralContainer
Fixture` trust `PROJECTION_MSSQL_CONN_STR` / a running `projection-mssql-warm`)
ŌĆö it manifested as 30 `SqlException` failures in the pure pool. Forcing a warm
container on a host that can't keep one alive is net-negative; warm
establishment stays owned by `session-start.sh` + `warm-sql.sh` (which
health-check + memory-cap), and the gate now **degrades gracefully** (skip, not
false-trip) when warm isn't available. Re-baselining was also rejected ŌĆö the
baseline is machine-shared; recording this container's variable cold timings
would be wrong for CI/dev.

**Witness.** Offline replay of the latest canary snapshot against the baseline:
0 hard regressions (the only over-threshold labels are `readside.*`, already
I/O-soft). Pure pool returned to 2829 / 0 after removing the zombie container.

---

**`DECISIONS 2026-06-06 (later)` ŌĆö Voice slice 1 landed: the Voice seam + the `code Ōćö copy` totality test; the two open sub-calls resolved (Core-purity ŌåÆ 1:1 projection-layer companion; the `Surface.fs` rename).**

Slice 1 of `THE_VOICE_INTEGRATION.md` ┬¦7 is built (plus the slice-2 stage scaffold), green on the full pure pool. What landed and what is now settled:

**1. The Voice seam exists (hybrid ┬¦5, mechanism 2).** `src/Projection.Cli/Voice.fs` is the code-keyed declarative catalog `Code ŌåÆ operator copy` for the flat LIVE lifecycle / verdict / config codes ŌĆö the boundary-side sibling of the engine's ubiquitous language. Each entry is a separable declarative `Copy` value (statement-first `Surface` built from the envelope payload, tagged with its `THE_VOICE.md` section), harvested into `Voice.all`; `TtyRenderer.buildSummaryView` now looks the verdict line up **by code** (`canary.diffEmpty`/`canary.divergence` ┬¦6, else `summary.runComplete` ┬¦3) rather than authoring `"SUCCEEDED"`/`"green"` inline. Codes voiced: the spine (`config.runStart`, `config.connectionResolved`, `summary.runComplete`, `config.validationFailed`) + the ┬¦13 Watch stage stream (`extract`/`profile`/`emit`.`started`/`.completed`, `summary.stageCompleted`) + the verdict proofs (`canary.*`). The open-ended `pipeline.config.*` / connection / permission error space is voiced by a routing **`errorFrame`** keyed on the code prefix (┬¦10/┬¦14), not one entry per `sprintf` subcode.

**2. The `code Ōćö copy` totality test (the sibling of `registered Ōćö executed`).** `tests/Projection.Tests/VoiceTotalityTests.fs` holds the copy honest by construction: every in-scope LIVE code has a `Voice.all` entry and every entry maps to an in-scope code (bidirectional); no entry voices a code the engine cannot emit (ŌŖå the known-emittable inventory); every entry cites a recognized `THE_VOICE.md` ┬¦; the `stageName` map is operator-shaped (never the internal verb) over the emitted stages; `errorFrame` routing is total. **The register is itself a test:** a mechanical banned-list guard (`THE_VOICE.md` ┬¦1 + ┬¦2.2) scans every voiced surface (filled + empty payload) and every error frame for pronouns / the antithesis tic / euphemism / drama / figurative terms / colloquialism / system-shout ŌĆö a line that breaks a rule fails the build.

**3. Core-purity sub-call ŌĆö RESOLVED: the 1:1 projection-layer companion** (the handoff's recommendation; operator-confirmed 2026-06-06). Per-site Voice copy for **pure-Core passes** lives in a `Voice.<Pass> Ōåö <Pass>` companion in the projection/CLI layer, **never** in `Projection.Core` ŌĆö the F#-pure-core / no-prose-in-Core commitment holds unbroken. **Load-bearing constraint that forces this:** the `View` / `Surface` substrate lives in the downstream `Projection.Cli` project, so `Projection.Core` *cannot* reference it (dependency direction) ŌĆö copy that produces a `View`/`Surface` value structurally cannot live in a Core module. "Declarations literally in Core" would therefore mean a Core-visible *structured-string* representation projected to `View` at the CLI boundary; the companion is cleaner and is the chosen form. No companion is needed yet: the move/gate/proof carriers are all Pipeline/CLI or boundary-projected (`EventProjection`); the pure-Core pass lift is the deferred slice-5 `DiagnosticEntry.Message` work (┬¦8 decision 4), which adopts this companion shape.

**4. The `Surface.fs` rename ŌĆö DONE.** `Surface.Essence` ŌåÆ `Surface.Statement`, `Surface.Dig` ŌåÆ `Surface.Substantiation` (and `Comparison.catalogEssence` ŌåÆ `Comparison.catalogStatement`), aligning the code vocabulary with the voice docs (┬¦1 rule 3 ŌĆö "legible statement, formal substantiation beneath"; "dig" retired). The diff-surface *copy strings* (`Comparison`, Act 2) are unchanged ŌĆö they are payload-shaped Act-2 move copy, voiced to the register in a later slice (mechanism 1); this rename only realigned the field vocabulary, per the "land it when you next touch `Surface`" instruction.

**5. What remains (the rest of the slice sequence).** Slice 2 (the streaming Watch render path + unifying `MigrationRun`/`TransferRun`/`EjectRun` onto the `LogSink` envelope spine ŌĆö only the **stage-code vocabulary** is scaffolded so far); slice 3 (aggregate-at-scale ┬¦12 + the timeline/ladder ┬¦8); slice 4 (lift `configError`/`ValidationError` copy + route `printErrors` through `Voice.errorSurface` + catch-and-translate exceptions + voice Argu `Usage`); slice 5 (the deferred typed `DiagnosticPayload` lift); and the cross-cutting mechanism-1 typed `toView`s for the ┬¦4 moves / ┬¦5 gates / ┬¦6 proofs (beside their `EventProjection` boundary projections; `TtyRenderer.buildGateSurface` is the existing model). Research maps for slices 2ŌĆō4 (per-run stageŌåÆ┬¦13 mapping, the LogSink-unification gap with file:line, the ┬¦12/┬¦8 accessor gaps, the error/config lift inventory, the mechanism-1 landing map, and the full test blast radius) were produced this session by a five-agent research pass and are captured in detail in **`THE_VOICE_BUILD_MAP.md`** ŌĆö the file:line execution layer beneath `THE_VOICE_INTEGRATION.md`. Read it before opening any of slices 2ŌĆō5.

**`DECISIONS 2026-06-06 (later, slice 2)` ŌĆö The streaming Watch (the live run, ┬¦13) lands over the already-emitted stage stream, with a minimum-dwell floor on the perceptual surface.**

The live-run surface (`THE_VOICE.md` ┬¦13 / `THE_STORYBOARD.md` Act 4) is built as `src/Projection.Cli/Watch.fs` ŌĆö a stage board that fills in as the run happens, opt-in via `--watch` on `full-export` (a real TTY required). Settled this slice:

**1. Watch is a *rendering* of the LogSink stage stream, never a second emit surface.** The board subscribes to the existing envelopes (`LogSink.addSubscriber`) ŌĆö exactly what channel 1 writes, after the verbosity/mute filter ŌĆö so the live surface and the NDJSON can never drift (the "one substrate, two lenses" discipline carried to the live leg). This slice voices the stage events `Compose.runWithConfig` **already** emits (`extract/profile/emit.started`, `summary.stageCompleted`); no new events (IR grows under evidence). The board reuses the ┬¦13 stage copy already in `Voice.all` (the gerund-in-progress while `Active`; the resultative + measured duration on `Done`).

**2. The minimum-dwell floor (operator concern; the load-bearing perceptual decision).** In a synchronous run the only live events are the discrete stage transitions, so a fast stage would flip `started ŌåÆ completed` faster than the eye can read. The floor is a **minimum inter-frame interval**: each visible board frame is held Ōēź the floor before the next is shown. Crucially it adds **only the remainder below the floor** ŌĆö a stage that already ran longer than the floor is never delayed (`Watch.dwellMs floor lastRenderAt now = max 0 (floor ŌłÆ (now ŌłÆ lastRenderAt))`) ŌĆö so the calm is bought by not flickering past a frame, *not* by slowing the run. **Stage granularity only:** the stream stays at the level worth representing as a stream (the handful of acts), not sub-stage micro-events that would themselves flicker. Default `120 ms`, overridable via `PROJECTION_WATCH_DWELL_MS` for perceptual tuning without a rebuild. The floor + board are pure + clock-injected, so the perceptual contract is unit-tested (`WatchTests`) without a TTY or a real sleep; the Spectre `Live` shell is the thin boundary.

**3. Scope + the deferred run-unification.** Wired to `full-export` only ŌĆö the one run that emits the stage stream today. Unifying `MigrationRun`/`TransferRun`/`EjectRun` onto the `LogSink` envelope spine (so they too stream), plus intra-stage progress + ETA, is the rest of slice 2 (mapped at `THE_VOICE_BUILD_MAP.md` ┬¦4.2/┬¦4.4). **Scope assumption recorded:** the dwell is enforced by the subscriber sleeping the emitting thread, which briefly holds the `LogSink` lock ŌĆö safe because the run is synchronous + single-threaded and channel 1 is nulled under `--watch` (no contention). A future concurrent/async emitter moves the dwell to a drain loop on a render thread (named in the Watch module header + build map ┬¦4.3). Default behavior is unchanged ŌĆö `--watch` is opt-in.

**`DECISIONS 2026-06-07` ŌĆö Voice slice 4 (errors as voice) + mechanism-1 gates (┬¦5 voiced over the closed `GateLabel` DU).**

Two voice increments landed green:

**1. Errors as voice (slice 4 core).** `Voice.errorsSurface : ValidationError list -> Surface` frames a refusal by its dominant (first) error through the ┬¦10/┬¦14 `errorFrame` routing, lists every located cause beneath (rule 3 ŌĆö the statement is legible, the codes ride in the substantiation, never on the lead). `TtyRenderer.renderErrors` renders it to stderr; `full-export`'s `ConfigInvalid` / `RunFailed` paths now route through it instead of `Console.Error.WriteLine "failed:"` + `[code] message`. **Codes are unchanged** ŌĆö the structured `config.validationFailed` NDJSON stays the machine channel; only the operator surface is voiced. **Deferred (the long tail):** lifting the ~30 `configError` `sprintf` messages into the catalog, migrating the ~48 `printErrors` call sites, voicing the Argu `Usage` strings, and the `ReadSide.fs:965` catch-and-translate ŌĆö all mapped in `THE_VOICE_BUILD_MAP.md` ┬¦6.1; the message text is already located/concrete, so the frame surfaces it correctly without the per-message lift.

**2. The ┬¦5 gates voiced (mechanism 1).** `Voice.gateStatement : Preflight.GateLabel -> Status ├Ś statement ├Ś action option` is the typed projection over the **closed** gate DU ŌĆö the gateŌćöcopy totality is the closed-DU analog of codeŌćöcopy (a `VoiceTotalityTests` test fails if a `GateLabel` variant is added without ┬¦5 copy). All eight labels now carry register-correct copy (the consequence as meaning + a plain active imperative ŌĆö `Approve ┬Ę Grant ┬Ę Map ┬Ę Check ┬Ę Correct ┬Ę Allow ┬Ę halt`), where before only `UndeclaredDestructiveChange` got distinct prose and the other seven collapsed to the bare axis name. `TtyRenderer.buildGateSurface` delegates to `Voice.gateSurface`. **Register correction caught by the banned-list guard:** the prior "<command> refused ŌĆö" gate framing used "refused" (a system-shout lean); a gate *stops to ask* (┬¦5), so the surface now leads directly with the consequence (the command context lives in the surrounding CLI narration). The true verb holds ŌĆö "This change **drops** a database object" (┬¦5 rule 5), not the prior "destroys structure" (drama).

**Still pending of "all five":** the ┬¦4-move / ┬¦6-proof typed `toView`s (mechanism 1, the migrate-preview + canary/verify surfaces ŌĆö `THE_VOICE_BUILD_MAP.md` ┬¦6.2); slice 3 (aggregate-at-scale ┬¦12 + timeline/ladder ┬¦8); the slice-2 run-unification (Migration/Transfer/Eject onto the spine) + intra-stage ETA; slice 5 (the typed `DiagnosticPayload` lift ŌĆö **deferred by design** behind a real consumer per IR-grows-under-evidence; the integration plan ┬¦8 decision 4 marks it optional/last). Pure pool green at each step.

**`DECISIONS 2026-06-07 (later)` ŌĆö Reconciling the voice work with the four-verb CLI re-envisioning; merged main, took its `Program.fs` verbatim.**

The CLI was re-envisioned on `main` (`THE_CLI.md`, PR #583) ŌĆö the ~16 verbs collapsed into **four** (`project` / `check` / `explain` / `seal`) over one `MovementSpec` (`Projection.Pipeline/MovementSpec.fs` + `MovementSurface.fs`), with the 16-verb Argu dispatch removed. This landed in parallel with the voice slices. The reconciliation:

1. **Take main on the CLI surface.** Merged `origin/main`; the only real conflict was `Program.fs` (main rewrote it; a slice-4 voice-error hunk auto-merged into a non-overlapping region). Per the operator's direction ("we're taking main on this one"), `Program.fs` is reset to main's exact version ŌĆö the voice-error wiring does not sneak in via the merge.
2. **The voice machinery is unchanged and survives.** Main kept and rewired `Voice.fs`, `Watch.fs` (now `--watch` on `project --to <folder> --config`), `TtyRenderer.fs`, `View.fs`, `Surface.fs`, the `LogSink` event spine, `EventProjection`, `Config`, `CatalogDiff`, `Episode`/`RunLedger`. The slice-1/2/4 voice surfaces (`Voice.lookup`/`errorsSurface`/`gateSurface`, `Watch`, `renderErrors`) all build green against main's `Program.fs` and stay test-covered (`VoiceTotalityTests`, `WatchTests`, `TtyRendererTests`).
3. **`THE_CLI.md` is a sibling, not a contradiction.** It writes in the `THE_VOICE.md` register (its ┬¦5 preview/refusal footers are voice-correct) and declares itself a sibling of the voice docs. The voice is the *register*; `THE_CLI` is the *surface* it speaks on.
4. **Voice surfaces are BUILT-but-not-wired into the new executors.** `errorsSurface`/`gateSurface`/`renderErrors` exist + tested but main's `Program.fs` still uses plain `printErrors` / inline refusal prose. Wiring them into the four executors (replacing those) is a **deliberate follow-up** with the team's CLI direction ŌĆö not snuck in via merge.
5. **Docs refactored toward the new surface.** `THE_VOICE_BUILD_MAP.md` gains a **┬¦0.5 reconciliation** (the old verb ŌåÆ new face map; what's superseded ŌĆö every `Program.fs` file:line ref, the run-type dispatch framing, the now-obsolete "voice Argu `Usage`" since the Argu files are deleted ŌĆö vs. what stays accurate ŌĆö every Pipeline/Core file:line, since main didn't touch them); the status table is updated (run-unification **subsumed** by `MovementSpec`; the ┬¦6 proofs now home on `check`, the ┬¦4 moves on `explain`). `THE_VOICE_INTEGRATION.md` gains a forward-pointer noting its ┬¦3.1 run types are now engine faces behind `MovementSpec`.

The CLI moved from sixteen verbs to four; the voice's substance (the seam, the `code Ōćö copy` totality, the dwell floor, the ┬¦12/┬¦8 rendering, the mechanism-1 `toView`s) is unchanged ŌĆö only the dispatch surface it renders on moved.

**`DECISIONS 2026-06-07 (errors wired)` ŌĆö The voice error surface is wired into the four-verb CLI: `printErrors` renders the ┬¦10/┬¦14 register; the `migrate` `REFUSED` shouts are revoiced.**

Following the reconciliation (`2026-06-07 (later)`), the voice error surface is now live on the new surface ŌĆö the convergence `THE_CLI.md` ┬¦5 asked for:

1. **`printErrors` ŌåÆ the voiced ┬¦10/┬¦14 surface (one change, total coverage).** `Program.fs`'s `printErrors` (the ~30-call-site helper every executor's error path routes through) now delegates to `TtyRenderer.renderErrorsTo` (the `Voice.errorsSurface` projection): a plain statement, the located cause + the code in the **substantiation** (never the code on the lead line), and the next move. This fixes the ┬¦10 violation (`  [<code>] <message>` led with the code) across `project` / `check` / `explain` / `seal` in one edit, no per-site churn. The prior `printErrorLine` is retired. **Codes unchanged** ŌĆö the `config.validationFailed` / `transfer.*` NDJSON is the machine channel, untouched; only the operator copy moved to the register. Smoke-tested end-to-end (a `pipeline.config.fileNotFound` renders `Ō£Ģ The configuration has a problemŌĆ”` / `problem ŌĆ”` / `code ŌĆ”` / `ŌåÆ CorrectŌĆ”`).

Surgical string fixes; no structural change to the refusal renderers.

3. **Still deliberate-follow-up (the ┬¦5 safety surface).** Routing `reportPreviewOutcome` (the migrate/project preview), `reportMigrationError`, and the connection/permission/tightening pre-flight renderers through `Voice.gateSurface`/a voiced preview footer is **not** done here ŌĆö those are payload-shaped (`MigrationError` / `GateRefusal` / `MigrationPreview`) and load-bearing for `THE_CLI.md` ┬¦5's preview-by-default safety model; rewiring them is the mechanism-1 ┬¦5 step, done carefully rather than via a blanket helper. The `Voice.gateSurface` projection (all 8 `GateLabel`s) is built + tested, ready to wire.

**`DECISIONS 2026-06-08 (later)` ŌĆö The voice backlog re-grounded against the North Star: vision first, mechanics subordinate.**

The first cut of `THE_VOICE_BACKLOG.md` read as a mechanical punch list ŌĆö file:line fixes with no North Star, the failure mode being that an agent initialized on it would produce banned-list-clean strings that miss the soul. Re-authored to lead with *why*, ingesting the supporting docs so they speak one language:

1. **A new ┬¦0 "The North Star."** Frames the whole work against `NORTH_STAR.md` (the engine is one adjunction `Ingest Ōłś Project = id`; fidelity is a *theorem the engine proves about itself*, not a property a human verifies) and `THE_INSTRUMENT.md` (the three layers Structure/Style/**Voice**; one essence infinitely diggable; the newcomer is the power user; *the instrument disappears*). The Voice is named as the **third layer** ŌĆö the one that renders the theorem as plain human confidence. The load-bearing reframe: **every raw render site is a seam where the instrument fails to disappear** ŌĆö the operator sees the apparatus (`%A`, `norm=`, `REFUSED`, a leaked exit code) instead of the truth. A `%A` at `:1167` is not a lint finding; it is the migration's *verdict* rendered as an internal DU dump.

2. **A new ┬¦1 "What 'done' means (the bar the guard cannot see)."** The `VoiceTotalityTests` banned-list guard is necessary, not sufficient ŌĆö it catches a pronoun/shout/`%A` but cannot tell whether a line is true, grounded, or kind. The bar: one register two velocities (┬¦0); essence on top, proof one level beneath (┬¦3/┬¦6/┬¦10); grounded authority (rule 4/8); ends on the move; the instrument disappears. **The felt arc (┬¦7) is the target, not the string.** The acceptance question for every item is "would the newcomer trust it on sight, would the master read it as a glance, and does the instrument disappear behind it?" ŌĆö not "does the guard pass?".

3. **Each wave reframed by the felt-arc moment it restores**, not just the ┬¦2.2 break it fixes: Wave 1 (gates) is *the consent moment ŌĆö trust earned in plain words*; Wave 2 (ŌĆ¢╬┤ŌĆ¢) is *the soul, said plain ŌĆö minimality is the faithfulness proof, today rendered as `norm=`*; Wave 3 (proofs) is *"This is the soul"*; Wave 4 (┬¦13) is *the rhythm names the next move*. The file:line mechanics are preserved in full beneath each ŌĆö vision-led, mechanics-subordinate.

4. **The handoff updated** with a "Read the *why* before you touch a string" section: the mandated reading order (`NORTH_STAR` ŌåÆ `THE_INSTRUMENT` ŌåÆ `THE_VOICE` ┬¦0/┬¦6/┬¦7/┬¦11/┬¦15) and the explicit warning that initializing on the file:line list alone produces guard-clean strings that miss the soul.

No code changed ŌĆö documentation grounding the rendering work in the vision. The mechanical inventory (the render-site classification, the renderer-per-gap, the wave order) is unchanged and intact; it now sits under the North Star rather than standing in for it.

**`DECISIONS 2026-06-08 (register-grounded)` ŌĆö THE_VOICE.md made operable in the backlog; the handoff boiled down.**

Two refinements so an executing agent cannot drift, and orients fast:

1. **The register is now in-doc and per-wave (`THE_VOICE_BACKLOG.md`).** A new ┬¦2 "The register in one screen" carries the twelve rules (┬¦1), the banned list (┬¦2.2), and the offŌåÆon calibration table (┬¦11) inline ŌĆö framed as "the corrections you will otherwise re-introduce by instinct" ŌĆö with the standing instruction to keep `THE_VOICE.md` open, the distillation being no substitute for the source. Every wave (0ŌĆō6) now carries a **Today ŌåÆ In register** worked example: the actual raw string at the site and the gold-standard voiced line derived from `THE_VOICE.md`, with the rule/section it satisfies (e.g. Wave 1's example is literally ┬¦11's worst case ŌĆö `migrate refused ŌĆö undeclared destructive change ┬Ę exit 9` ŌåÆ `The IX_Order_Stale index will be dropped. No data is lost, but the drop is not auto-reversible. ŌåÆ Approve the removal, or halt.`). The target string shape is unambiguous at each site, so producing a guard-clean-but-soulless line is no longer the path of least resistance.

Same content, a fraction of the read.

**`DECISIONS 2026-06-08 (legibility axiom)` ŌĆö The substantiation translates too: algebra/erudite jargon out, domain technicality in; the bar is the technical layperson.**

A register clarification the worked examples forced into the open. The first cut of the backlog's `Today ŌåÆ In register` examples put arcane tokens in the deeper `beneath:` substantiation lines (`ŌĆ¢╬┤ŌĆ¢ = 312 = CDC capture count`, `residual Ōłģ ┬Ę Ingest Ōłś Project = id`, `CDC = 0 ┬Ę zero ALTERs`, `residual ŌēĀ Ōłģ`, `ŽĆ Ōłś Žā Ōēł id`). This is **axiomatically wrong** by `THE_VOICE.md` ┬¦2.1 ("The boundary translates ŌĆö *always*") and the ┬¦11 calibration `ŌĆ¢╬┤ŌĆ¢ norm Ō¢▓ 3` ŌåÆ `Total changes: 3`, which holds *at every depth*, not just on the lead. The substantiation is the statement **made rigorous in plain words**, never the statement trailed by its glyphs ŌĆö the depth is more plain precise detail, not a descent into notation.

The governing bar, stated for execution: **every rendered string ŌĆö statement and substantiation alike ŌĆö must be legible to a technical layperson** (a developer or DBA who knows databases but nothing of this engine's internals). The line, sharpened with the operator: **algebra and erudite jargon are out; domain concreteness and purposeful technicality are in.**
- **Out (never rendered):** `ŌĆ¢╬┤ŌĆ¢ Ōłģ Ōłś Ōēł`, norm, residual, commuting square, isomorphism, torsor, quotient, surrogate, `Ingest Ōłś Project` ŌĆö recast in plain/domain terms (rows changed ┬Ę nothing left over ┬Ę the database matches the model ┬Ę one difference remains).
- **In (stays ŌĆö sharpens, not obscures):** CDC, ALTER/ADD/DROP, idempotent, index, column, schema, the exit code. A DBA knows them; they are concrete and purposeful, not category theory.

Captured in the backlog as: ┬¦1's bar sentence; a ┬¦2 "axiom, stated for execution" callout with an algebraŌåÆplain table and an explicit "stays" list; ┬¦1 rule-2 rewritten ("the proof is plain too"); every `beneath:` example purged of algebra (the ┬¦6 gold statements ŌĆö including "Confirmed idempotentŌĆ”" ŌĆö restored, since idempotent/CDC/ALTER are permitted); and the one surviving formula (the ┬¦0 adjunction law) explicitly tagged "the one piece of algebra in this documentŌĆ” never rendered to the operator; the thing the Voice translates *from*." Verified mechanically: no algebra token appears in any rendered Ō£ō example or `beneath:` line. No code changed.

**`DECISIONS 2026-06-08 (handoff reified)` ŌĆö `HANDOFF_VOICE_2026_06_06.md` fully reified into the single complete brief.**

Per the operator ("fully reify it now ŌĆö this one document should be everything the agent needs to get going"), the voice handoff is rewritten from an anchor-letter-plus-addendum into a **self-contained brief** that orients a fresh agent end to end and maps the full corpus. The brief itself obeys the legibility axiom: the only algebra it contains is the engine-internal law (explicitly tagged "the one piece of algebra in this briefŌĆ” never shown to the operator; the thing the Voice translates from"), the deeper-substrate concept names in the doc map, and the legibility axiom naming the banned set ŌĆö none in anything representing rendered operator copy. No code changed.

**`DECISIONS 2026-06-08 (intent gate)` ŌĆö The `--go` / `PROJECTION_ALLOW_EXECUTE` consent refusal voices through a flat `gate.intent` code, not a `Preflight.GateLabel` variant.**

Resolved while wiring Voice Wave 1 (`HANDOFF_VOICE_2026_06_06.md` ┬¦8). The two-gate consent model (`--go` states intent; `PROJECTION_ALLOW_EXECUTE=1` arms the live write ŌĆö ┬¦5/┬¦7) had no home in the engine's closed `Preflight.GateLabel` DU. Two options: add a `Preflight.IntentNotStated` variant (keeping the closed-DU gateŌćöcopy totality covering it), or voice it through a flat `gate.intent` `errorSurface` code. **Chosen: the flat code.** The intent gate is a **CLI-surface consent concern, not an engine pre-flight** ŌĆö the engine's `Preflight` DU classifies refusals it discovers by probing the substrate (connection, grant, tightening, CDC); the arming gate is a property of the CLI invocation (an env var + the `--go`/`--execute` flag), discovered before any engine call. Putting it in the engine's closed DU would conflate the apparatus's consent layer with the engine's pre-flight axes. The flat code routes through `Voice.errorFrame` (the open-ended ┬¦10/┬¦14 routing, the same mechanism the `pipeline.config.*` / connection / permission codes use), so no engine DU widens and the gateŌćöcopy totality stays exactly the closed `Preflight.GateLabel` set. Copy: `View.Warn` (a consent pause, not a hard failure) ŌĆö "A live write requires PROJECTION_ALLOW_EXECUTE=1 in the environment." ŌåÆ "Set PROJECTION_ALLOW_EXECUTE=1, then re-run." Wired at all four `--execute`/`--go` refusal sites (transfer, migrate in-place, migrate cross-substrate, synthetic), each unchanged at exit 7; the totality test extends to cover `gate.intent` in the routing-total and error-frame-banned-list checks plus a focused assertion the frame names the arming variable. No code (event) changed ŌĆö pure rendering.

---

**`DECISIONS 2026-06-09` ŌĆö Agent test-execution protocol: run focused tests directly; never `pgrep`-guard a run.**

Codified after this session burned real time on three self-inflicted "hangs" that were never the code. The protocol, going forward:

1. **To run one class/method warm, invoke it directly:** `scripts/test.sh focus "<FullyQualifiedName-substring>"`. Do **not** gate it behind a `pgrep` / `until` wait loop. Two traps make such guards wait forever:
   - `pgrep -f "dotnet test"` (and `pkill -f "test.sh focus"`) match the **guard's own command line** ŌĆö the pattern string is literally in it ŌĆö so the command waits on / kills *itself* (observed: a `pkill -f "test.sh focus"` that exited 144 by self-SIGTERM; an `until ! pgrep -f "dotnet test"` that never advanced).
   - `pgrep -x dotnet` matches **persistent MSBuild node-reuse workers** ŌĆö idle `dotnet` (MSBuild.dll) nodes that linger ~15 min after a build ŌĆö so `until ! pgrep -x dotnet` never proceeds even when no test is running.
2. **Don't pipe a long run through `| tail -N`** when you want to watch it ŌĆö `tail -N` buffers to EOF, so the output file stays empty mid-run and looks hung. Stream to the file and `grep`/`wc -l` it for progress; reserve `| tail` for the final summary only.
3. **If a build genuinely seems stuck, suspect orphaned MSBuild nodes**, not the test. `MSBUILDDISABLENODEREUSE=1` avoids spawning reusable nodes; `pkill -9 dotnet` clears stuck ones. A warm focused Docker run lands in **seconds** (e.g. the migrate stage-stream test: 7s warm) ŌĆö if one appears to "hang," it is almost always the guard or buffering, not the test.
4. **Pure pool stays the inner loop** (`scripts/test.sh fast`); the pools never run concurrently (the existing OOM discipline). The warm SQL container is owned by `scripts/warm-sql.sh` (`restart` to revive a dead one ŌĆö a dead `projection-mssql-warm` is the cause of a batch of `SqlException: Could not open a connection` failures while `PROJECTION_MSSQL_CONN_STR` is still set).

This pairs with the "Tiered test runner" and "Test-failure capture protocol" disciplines (CLAUDE.md operating-disciplines table) ŌĆö those say *which* pool and *how to read failures*; this says *how to invoke a focused run without hanging the loop*.

---

**`DECISIONS 2026-06-09` ŌĆö The Voice implementation chapter: the backlog is rendered; the dynamic frontier is open and honest.**

Closes the `HANDOFF_VOICE_2026_06_06.md` brief. The substantive resolutions (the narrative lives in `HANDOFF_VOICE_CLOSE_2026_06_09.md` + the commit trail):

1. **The register is LIVE on every operator surface.** The seven waves shipped: no `%A` DU dump, no system-shout lead, no algebra (`norm=`/`ŌĆ¢╬┤ŌĆ¢`), no leaked `SsKey`/`OS_KIND` reaches the operator anywhere in the CLI ŌĆö verified by a mechanical sweep. The gates (`renderGate` over the closed `Preflight.GateLabel`), the ┬¦6 proofs, the ┬¦4 moves, the ┬¦10 errors, the ┬¦13 lifecycle all render through the catalog/`View`.

2. **The intent gate is a flat `gate.intent` code, not an engine DU variant** (recorded 2026-06-08; wired in Wave 1) ŌĆö the engine's closed `GateLabel` DU stays exactly the gateŌćöcopy totality set.

3. **The declared-loss gate names the REAL levers** (`--allow-drops` / `--declare-drop`), corrected from the idle copy's non-existent `--declare-loss`/`--declare-all`. A gate's action must be executable.

4. **The live Watch is the rendering of the stage stream, wired across the run verbs.** `Watch.renderWatch` (pre-seeded `Pending` arc; the minimum-dwell floor) is wired onto `project` (bundle + load), `migrate --execute`, `migrate-with-data`, `transfer --execute`, and `project --deploy`. The executors stream the *existing* stage codes at their phase boundaries (`MigrationRun.execute` ŌåÆ emit/deploy/canary; `TransferRun.writePlan` ŌåÆ load) plus a new `summary.stageProgress` + `LogSink.recordStageProgress` for intra-stage progress. **Codes never changed** ŌĆö only new emit sites + the one progress event. Verified live (Docker): the migrate streamed `emit/deploy/canary .started ŌåÆ stageCompleted` and `summary.stageProgress done 1ŌåÆ2ŌåÆ3`; the data load streamed `load.started` + per-table progress.

5. **The honest estimate degrades by construction** (`Watch.etaText`): none before the first item or at the last; an estimate only when a rate can be projected; `progressText` is total over `Total` ŌĆö an unknown denominator is a plain count-up ("142 applied"), never "142 of 0" (┬¦13).

6. **Per-table deploy progress is NOT honestly achievable, and was not faked.** `project --deploy` is one aggregated SQL batch (`runFromCatalog` ŌåÆ `runEphemeral(aggregateSsdt)`; even `executeStream` flushes DDL once, on an interleaved `InsertRow` or at the end) ŌĆö batching is a deliberate perf choice. A per-table counter would show "300 of 300" while the single batch is still executing, exactly the misstated bar ┬¦13 forbids. The deploy verb gets an honest live *stage* (Applying ŌåÆ Deploy complete); per-unit progress lives where there is a true per-unit boundary ŌĆö the data-load loop.

7. **The ┬¦14 setup readback is the last latent surface, now live.** `projection setup [--conn <ref>]` reads back history / live-writes / board / bench, recommends an unset ledger (never scolds), and with `--conn` probes the target's reachability + the ALTER grant (reusing the verified pre-flight machinery). Verified live against the warm container.

The locked register decisions (`HANDOFF_VOICE_2026_06_06.md` Appendix A) held throughout ŌĆö stative/agentless, the legibility axiom (algebra out, domain in), the true verb, no pronouns. The pure-Core boundary held (all prose in `Projection.Cli`; the NDJSON event contract unchanged).
